MeshCore
📚 Forums / software-development / MeshCore One - An open source MeshCore client for iOS/iPadOS
avion 2026-01-08 3:07 p.m.
Announcing the beta of MeshCore One (MC1), an open source MeshCore client written in Swift. Notable features:
  • Line of Sight tool that allows adding a repeater in the path to see if adding one would make the path viable
  • Trace Path that allows saving trace paths, batching trace paths, and seeing statistics
  • Link previews
  • Reactions
  • Inline gif/image previews
  • Block/favorite contacts
  • Mute/favorite chats
  • Battery OCV curve presets and custom tuning
  • WiFi node support
  • CLI
  • Full iPad support with split views Future features:
  • Room management
  • Guest repeater management view
  • macOS support
  • Import/Export config settings
  • Translations What doesn't work:
  • You tell me! (where my xda oldies at)
  • iOS/iPadOS 18 compatibility may be rough. I don’t have a physical device in 18, so there are probably bugs I haven’t caught in the simulator.
  • It's possible the app has bugs in busy networks. I don't have many nodes/activity happening where I am so couldn't stress test reliably. Let me know if you run into any bugs or have feature ideas, I'd love to hear them GitHub repo: https://github.com/Avi0n/MeshCoreOne
TestFlight link: https://testflight.apple.com/join/YjGNDTaE OS requirements:
  • iOS >= 18.0
  • iPadOS >= 18.0 Disclaimer: This app was mostly built with AI and I am not a professional developer.
 (edited)
15

mcarper 2026-01-08 7:41 p.m.
@inklit check out the video
avion
Announcing the beta of MeshCore One (MC1), an open source MeshCore client written in Swift. Notable features:
  • Line of Sight tool that allows adding a repeater in the path to see if adding one would make the path viable
  • Trace Path that allows saving trace paths, batching trace paths, and seeing statistics
  • Link previews
  • Reactions
  • Inline gif/image previews
  • Block/favorite contacts
  • Mute/favorite chats
  • Battery OCV curve presets and custom tuning
  • WiFi node support
  • CLI
  • Full iPad support with split views Future features:
  • Room management
  • Guest repeater management view
  • macOS support
  • Import/Export config settings
  • Translations What doesn't work:
  • You tell me! (where my xda oldies at)
  • iOS/iPadOS 18 compatibility may be rough. I don’t have a physical device in 18, so there are probably bugs I haven’t caught in the simulator.
  • It's possible the app has bugs in busy networks. I don't have many nodes/activity happening where I am so couldn't stress test reliably. Let me know if you run into any bugs or have feature ideas, I'd love to hear them GitHub repo: https://github.com/Avi0n/MeshCoreOne
TestFlight link: https://testflight.apple.com/join/YjGNDTaE OS requirements:
  • iOS >= 18.0
  • iPadOS >= 18.0 Disclaimer: This app was mostly built with AI and I am not a professional developer.
 (edited)
Jude 2026-01-09 12:32 a.m.

avion 2026-01-09 12:33 a.m.
Thanks haha. I know how to make dumb Discord/Telegram bots in Python so programming isn't completely foreign to me. But definitely not a SWE. I just cosplay as one with Claude
avion 2026-01-09 1:03 p.m.
0.3.0 is now available on TestFlight Features
  • Added beta support for WiFi firmware devices
  • Added debug log export feature in Settings Bug Fixes
  • Fixed issue where BLE devices would not re-connect if they were out of range or off for a while
  • Fixed potential issue where chat view would not refresh when services reconnect
  • Added missing public channel key
  • Improved channel sync reliability
1:04 p.m.
0.3.1 is awaiting TestFlight review approval and adds the Clock Sync command for repeaters and a small change to the current "Syncing..." pill to show you if it's syncing channels or messages.

avion 2026-01-09 2:04 p.m.
The reason it's at the bottom is because when you add repeaters, the list in the Outbound Path grows and then pushes down the Available Repeaters list which I found annoying. So I moved it to the bottom. But maybe the annoyance of the list moving after selecting a repeater is better than not knowing the list is there at the bottom.
Robowarrior834 2026-01-09 2:05 p.m.
so far i like the app, feels like the meshtastic app. Any plans for a gui path tool like the offical app?
avion 2026-01-09 2:05 p.m.

2:06 p.m.

Whitetail Wise 2026-01-09 9:06 p.m.
Test flight runs well. I’m curious though, what’s the biggest features this has over the regular MeshCore app?
9:08 p.m.
Tracepath and los very nice
avion 2026-01-09 9:10 p.m.
Thanks for testing! So far, nothing really sets it apart except for the Line of Sight tool. And that is by design. My goal with this app is to create an Apple native experience version of the official MeshCore app. No fancy additions unless people really want them. Extensions to features such as the line of sight tool are good too. And hopefully PocketMesh has more reliable bluetooth connections than the official MeshCore iOS app.
Whitetail Wise 2026-01-09 9:10 p.m.
Sounds good to me. Thanks for explaining and great work
1

[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-11 1:10 a.m.
Installed it this evening. Very nice. At replies seem not quite right (it included too much text), but I was able to send messages without issue. I'll play with it more tomorrow.
avion 2026-01-11 1:13 a.m.
Thanks for testing! Let me know what you think after playing with it more. Open to any bugs reports, features you miss, etc. Yes, that was a personal decision. Didn't like how you couldn't tell which message someone was replying to. But since there's no official way to do a reply, that was was the best I could come up with. But I'm unsure if I should keep it in. (edited)
Mike / N2QDK 2026-01-11 8:17 a.m.
I had the same initial reaction, then realized intent behind. Makes since as there’s no universal unique ID for individual messages (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-01-11 1:24 p.m.
I just had an interesting thing happen. I had my radio connected via Liam's app, and then I launched PocketMesh and... they both connected at the same time. Some messages ended up in PocketMesh, some ended up in Liam's app, and others ended up in both. I've never seen that happen with any other app or the meshcore-cli/meshcore_py. Just something I wanted to flag for you.
3
avion 2026-01-11 1:29 p.m.

11:56 p.m.
0.3.3 has been approved. Whats New:
  • Suppress new message push notifications when connecting to a device
  • New "Connecting..." pill appears when PocketMesh is trying to connect to a device
  • Add more debug logging. Now saves logs between app restarts
  • In repeater admin settings, changed Max Flood Hops from a stepper to an input field
  • In repeater admin settings, changed "Force Advertisement" text to "Send Advert" Bug Fixes:
  • Allow passwords longer than 15 chars in room/repeater logins with educational message about truncation.
  • Fix messages getting stuck in "Sending..." state
  • Fix iPad crash when presenting Share Sheet in the Trace Path tool
avion
How many hops away is it?
NeoPhaze 2026-01-12 12:02 a.m.
0 hop
1

NeoPhaze 2026-01-12 12:02 a.m.

I haven’t put very much time into the repeater admin commands yet. I’ll work on making it more stable soon. I may switch to official app’s way of having to tap to load every field. Would make it more reliable to load and would send less traffic (edited)

NeoPhaze 2026-01-12 12:13 a.m.

1
avion 2026-01-14 2:49 a.m.
Version 0.4.0 has been submitted for review! Release notes below. What's New
  • Revamped Contacts Tab: Now rebranded as "Nodes", featuring category switching between Contacts and Network devices (repeaters & rooms). Also added sorting e.g. last heard advert.
  • Updated Map: Visualize clusters with numbered indicators and added map layers.
  • Batch Trace Mode Introduced: Trace Path tool has a new batch processing mode, including averaging for better trace insights.
  • Refined SNR Attribution: Transitioned from sender to receiver attribution in Trace Path SNR values.
  • Unread Mentions: A new button in chats scrolls you to your unread mentions.
  • Mute Conversations Easily: Swipe any conversation to mute it.
  • Block Contacts: Swipe a Contact in the Node list to block unwanted interactions. This will delete all chats with the Contact and prevent their messages from showing in channels.
  • Mention Picker: Type "@" to quickly select contacts to mention.
  • Hashtag Channel Integration: Tap hashtag channels in messages to join or create them on the fly.
  • More Accurate Battery Percentage: Automatically set OCV battery curve based on device manufacturer during first pair.
  • Enhanced Pill Status Indicators: Added more pill statuses including Connecting, Connected, and Disconnected.
  • Quick Device Connection: Tap the pill in Disconnected state to open the Connect Device sheet from anywhere in the app.
  • Intelligent Connection Detection: Detect and prevent connections if BLE device is connected to another app.
 (edited)
2
1
2:49 a.m.
  • Proactive Battery Alerts: Receive low battery notifications at 20%, 10%, and 5% thresholds.
  • New Repeater OCV Option: Added Seeed Solar Node to the Repeater OCV menu in the repeater telemetry screen.
  • Expanded Logging: Added more detailed logging for Bluetooth connections and repeater commands.
  • Extended Debug Logs: Increased debug log retention from 1 hour to 24 hours to better capture Bluetooth issues.
  • Offline Debug Export: Now export debug logs even without a connected device.
  • Polished UI/UX: Refined interfaces for a smoother experience (thanks Mike!). Bug Fixes
  • Fixed numpad positioning issues on iPad for WiFi connection sheets.
  • Attempt at improving reliability of repeater CLI command/response handling.
  • Fixed unresponsive Done button when editing Repeater Name field.
  • Addressed potential stuck "Syncing" pill that required force quit to resolve.
 (edited)
2:52 a.m.
Thank you to everyone who submitted TestFlight feedback and created GitHub issues
2
Mike / N2QDK 2026-01-14 6:45 a.m.
Tremendous job @avion !!! This is now my preferred client already!

ferdia 2026-01-14 7:55 a.m.
same:) Anyone else getting this error on macOS? Bluetooth definitely enabled and allowed for the app Cannot show device picker. Please check that Bluetooth is enabled and the app has permission.
kri5irk 2026-01-14 8:26 a.m.
The trace path features are awesome. Having a bookmark AND statistics is great for testing new antennas and mounting locations. The batch feature is also awesome and gives you good statistical data on the links really quickly.
3
1
ferdia
same:) Anyone else getting this error on macOS? Bluetooth definitely enabled and allowed for the app Cannot show device picker. Please check that Bluetooth is enabled and the app has permission.
avion 2026-01-14 10:17 a.m.
macOS isn’t supported yet due to differences in how Bluetooth pairing works. But I’m looking into it! Technically it will work on macOS if you use a WiFi node. But I haven’t tested it so there are likely many things that are glitchy/broken.
1
avion
macOS isn’t supported yet due to differences in how Bluetooth pairing works. But I’m looking into it! Technically it will work on macOS if you use a WiFi node. But I haven’t tested it so there are likely many things that are glitchy/broken.

avion 2026-01-16 7:43 p.m.
PocketMesh 0.4.1 has been submitted to Apple for review There are 3 things to really pay attention to and test for this version: 1. All things syncing. Changes were made aiming for a rock solid background connection, but it needs testing. Does it sync contacts correctly? Channels? When backgrounded? Disconnected/reconnected? etc. 2. Chat view. Is the lag from lots of new messages gone? Are there any new visual glitches? 3. There was a background refactor in this build to unify a lot of duplicated code between iPad and iOS. Notice any weird visual inconsistencies/glitches? What Changed
  • Chat: Switch color to blue for all outgoing message bubbles
  • Chat: Add ability to copy path in the Repeat Details section
  • Map: See the Last Advert in node details
  • Radio: Add/adjust Australia radio presets
  • Sync: Now only does a partial contact sync while in background, no channel sync. Channel sync will occur upon opening the app.
  • Trace Path: After loading a saved path, automatically scroll down to the Run Trace button
Bug Fixes
  • Fix choppy scrolling in chats when many messages were received
  • Fix reconnect sync hang
  • Fix sync failure when app was backgrounded
  • Fix crash when attempting to type a password for a repeater on the Map tab
  • Fix non-standard firmware version formats not displaying in repeater settings
  • Fix Jump to Path button not disappearing on iPad
  • Fix inability to save a path that was previously saved but deleted
 (edited)
2
1
1
Whitetail Wise 2026-01-16 7:45 p.m.
Wicked
1
avion 2026-01-16 7:45 p.m.
Thank you again to everyone who submitted feedback and GH issues. Keep 'em coming!
1
avion 2026-01-19 2:41 a.m.
0.4.2 has been submitted to Apple for review! In addition to continuing to keep an eye out for Syncing bugs (especially Contacts) and chat view UI glitches from 0.4.1, keep an eye out for stuck pill statuses, pills that should be there but aren't, etc. In this release, I refactored the Status Pill's code to be simpler and easier to maintain. But this also means I could have missed some edge cases again. Thank you in advance! What Changed
  • Chats: Swipe a DM/Channel/Room to the right to Favorite
  • Status Pill: Now has animations instead of just appearing/disappearing instantly
  • Status Pill: Removed Channels and Contacts from the pill status as it felt too busy for an Apple app
  • New "Send Again" button in long press menu for messages that failed to send or had 0 heard repeats
  • Added logging for radio settings (Frequency, Spreading Factor, etc.)
Bug Fixes
  • Fixed a bug where swiping to delete a contact would cause a crash
  • Fixed a bug where occasionally tapping on a node that was clustered would automatically zoom you out
  • Fixed crashes during transitional states (closing sheets, backing out, opening the app launcher)
  • iPad now reflects the correct radio settings if you have Advanced Settings open and are looking at the Radio section in the main Settings panel at the same time
  • Fix rooms not updating badge notifications reliably
  • Fix room notifications not clearing after reading
  • Fix rooms not sorting by last received time
  • Fix room message bubble text being black instead of white
As always, thank you for all the TestFlight feedback reports and GitHub issues. I really appreciate them (edited)
2
1
avion 2026-01-19 11:55 a.m.
0.4.3 has been submitted to Apple for review. This is a small bug fix release for issues found in 0.4.2 Bug Fixes
  • Fix bug where heard repeats were not showing under sent messages.
  • Fix bug on iOS 18 where the labels button, top toolbar, and bottom tab bar on the Map tab were invisible
 (edited)

  • Long press on an incoming message to see the path it took. Can copy the path.

avion 2026-01-24 2:23 a.m.

I was pondering whether or not to do an @mention or not if the user pressed "Reply" on a channel message notification. And then...forgot about it lol. But you reminded me so I'll add this soon! But for DMs, if you see the banner come in, you can swipe down on it to Reply or Mark as Read. In the Notification Center, you can Reply or Mark as Read by long pressing on the notif. Works on Apple Watch notifs too. (edited)

  • Support auto-updating node location from device's built-in GPS
  • Add filtering, sorting, and clear options to Discover page
2
avion 2026-01-25 4:28 p.m.
PocketMesh 0.5.0 has been submitted to TestFlight for review!

What's Changed

  • Multilingual Support: Added localization for 7 new languages: Dutch, French, German, Polish, Russian, Spanish, and Ukrainian. These were translated by AI, so expect some many quirks. Want to fix them or add more? Check out TRANSLATIONS.md on GitHub. PRs are super welcome!
  • Noise Floor Monitoring Tool: Introduced a new tool to monitor noise floor with handy statistics.
  • Total Distance in Trace Path: Now view the total distance for successful traces in the Trace Path tool.
  • Repeater Input Field: Added a new field in Trace Path to easily type or paste repeaters.
  • Repeater Favorites: Added a new toggle in Trace Path to only show your favorite repeaters.
  • Map Mode in Trace Path: Check out the new Map mode in the Trace Path tool!
  • Message Path Viewer: Long press on a received message to see its incoming path (requires your node to be connected to PM at the time).
  • Pinned Favorite Chats: Favorites are now pinned right at the top of your chat list.
  • Reply From Channel Notifications: Added "Reply" to notification banners so you can reply without opening the app. Works on Apple Watch notifications too!
  • Enhanced Sender Names: In Channels and Rooms, sender names are now bigger, bolder, and more colorful.
  • Grouped Messages: Subsequent messages from the same sender are now neatly grouped together.
  • Expanded Mention Picker: Now includes recent channel senders for quicker mentions.
  • Refreshed Color Palette: Updated the overall color palette.
  • Flexible Repeater Login: Allow empty password login for ACL authentication.
  • Auto-Zoom Location Setting: When setting node or repeater location, it auto-zooms to your GPS spot if location permissions are enabled.
  • Smart Button Disabling: Buttons across the app are disabled when the radio is disconnected.
  • Offline Data Browsing: Browse data from previously connected devices even when disconnected.
  • Haptic Feedback on Copies: Added haptic feedback to all copy/paste buttons for better feel.
  • Polished UI/UX: A little smoother animation here, a little less jank there. ## Bug Fixes
  • Fixed crash by validating lat/lon for contacts on the map.
  • Fixed crash by clamping initial camera zoom-out on the map.
  • Fixed rooms not sorting by last message received date in chats.
  • Fixed rooms lingering in the chats list after node deletion.
  • Fixed ability to create duplicate hashtag channels in chats.
  • Fixed contacts incorrectly matching in the "Repeat Details" sheet in chats.
  • Attempted fix for location not saving in settings, with added logging for troubleshooting.
  • Fixed inability to clear a location from the node in settings.
  • Fixed some repeaters not showing their name in Trace Results in tools.
  • Fixed chat input bar ignoring floating keyboard on iPad.
  • Fixed nodes not deleting from the database if removed in another app during sync.
  • Fixed incomplete contact sync by performing a full sync on first or manual connection. I'm not super happy with the input field UX in Trace Path. But it works and is important for busy networks so I wanted to get it out ASAP even with its quirks. I've been getting lots of great feedback, crash reports, and feature requests. Thank you! I appreciate each and every one of them
(edited)
PocketMesh/TRANSLATIONS.md at main · Avi0n/PocketMesh
A native MeshCore client for iOS & iPadOS. Contribute to Avi0n/PocketMesh development by creating an account on GitHub.

avion 2026-01-26 7:52 p.m.
0.5.0 has been approved! Go forth and find those bugs! And enjoy the new features too I guess :p
avion 2026-01-27 2:03 a.m.
0.5.1 has been submitted to TestFlight for review! What's Changed
  • See incoming hops and path right under incoming messages. To enable, go to Settings > Advanced
  • Enable pairing support for Whisper OS devices
  • "Node Discovered" notifications now tell you what type of node it is e.g. "New Repeater Discovered"
Bug Fixes
  • Fix low battery notifications not firing if connecting after passing a percentage threshold
  • Fix blank icon for the Noise Floor tool on iOS/iPadOS 18
 (edited)

megadimich
PocketMesh has a design similar to Apple, which is pleasant and attractive. All menu items are harmonious, and the font size is very suitable. It connects to Room Server immediately. The disadvantage is that Room Server does not provide information about message delivery.
avion 2026-01-27 8:12 p.m.
Yeah, rooms need some love from me. I'll plan to have delivery confirmation out in 0.6.0!
1
1
1
megadimich
Thank you for the Ukrainian language
avion 2026-01-27 8:13 p.m.
You're welcome! I'm sure there are mistakes. If you want to create a PR to fix them, I'd be very appreciative!
megadimich
Is it possible to enable sending GIF files, as in meshcore_open?
avion 2026-01-27 8:18 p.m.
hm... I'm hesitant to add anything that will require certain apps to see/interact with. But if more people ask for it, I may consider adding it under advanced options. The other thing for me to consider is rate limits. I think meshcore open uses giphy, and the free API key is rate limited to 100 searches/API calls an hour. Which PocketMesh probably won't hit anytime soon with its little over 100 users, but if it eventually does, I'd have to start paying out of pocket for it. It also is not good practice to include the API key in the source code, so I'd have to spin up a small hetzner server or something similar to act as a proxy where the actual API key lives. Which would cost about $5/mo. (edited)
1
1
8:23 p.m.
Long way of saying, it is possible to integrate gifs into PocketMesh. But I'm hesitant to, due to financial and MeshCore interoperability reasons.
8:24 p.m.
But I probably will if enough people want it. (edited)
1
avion
You're welcome! I'm sure there are mistakes. If you want to create a PR to fix them, I'd be very appreciative!
megadimich 2026-01-28 7:05 a.m.
Everything is fine. But there are a few untranslated blocks.
avion
But I probably will if enough people want it. (edited)

11:59 p.m.
To those who use PocketMesh as their main MeshCore app, hold off on upgrading your companion nodes to v1.12. PocketMesh is not compatible with the new bluetooth changes and will not reliably sync all your nodes. If you’ve already updated to v1.12, I suggest either downgrading temporarily or using Liam’s app until I get PocketMesh updated.
2
avion 2026-01-30 8:11 a.m.
Fix applied, now going through internal testing. If all goes well, I’ll submit it to Apple and you all should get access roughly 1 business day later. It’ll be a little earlier than anticipated, but the build will be 0.6.0. Because it’s early, it’ll have less features/fixes than normal, but it does include the much anticipated CLI tool! (edited)

Really liking your work avion! Thanks for all the effort. Installed it today and still learning. So there are a few questions. I‘m writing to a friend of mine (DM) and it tells me 2 Hops (which is true) but at left side of 2 Hops still is shown direct… do i miss something? Is it intentional? Second Question: did you already implmented the „auto reset path“-Function for the fifth sending? If yes - i‘m blind. What i‘m missing (at least my opinion) is being able to see how many times it tried to send a message like in Liams App. I hope you understand what i‘m meaning. And maybe its a bug or i made a mistake: i connected to my already used T1000E with PocketMesh. Like you mentioned above in a release note, it should auto set the battery-curve. But it didnt. If i can help you from my side to fix it, just tell me how and i will provide any Information needed since i‘m really liking what i see so far! Keep going man! You‘re doing great! (edited)
Bullit
Really liking your work avion! Thanks for all the effort. Installed it today and still learning. So there are a few questions. I‘m writing to a friend of mine (DM) and it tells me 2 Hops (which is true) but at left side of 2 Hops still is shown direct… do i miss something? Is it intentional? Second Question: did you already implmented the „auto reset path“-Function for the fifth sending? If yes - i‘m blind. What i‘m missing (at least my opinion) is being able to see how many times it tried to send a message like in Liams App. I hope you understand what i‘m meaning. And maybe its a bug or i made a mistake: i connected to my already used T1000E with PocketMesh. Like you mentioned above in a release note, it should auto set the battery-curve. But it didnt. If i can help you from my side to fix it, just tell me how and i will provide any Information needed since i‘m really liking what i see so far! Keep going man! You‘re doing great! (edited)
avion 2026-02-01 2:09 p.m.
Hey thanks for testing PocketMesh! Really happy to hear you're enjoying it so far I'll answer your questions below.
  • The message header will tell you whether the path is Direct or Flood. As long as the route is not Flood, it is considered Direct. If it is 0 hop, you will see "Direct 0 hop".
  • Yes, the auto reset path is on by default. If the contact had a direct path but the path no longer works, it will switch to Flood after 3 attempts. Then it will try Flood twice. You should be able to see the retry status underneath the message and in the header underneath the contact's name. Once all 5 tries are exhausted, the message will turn red and let you know it failed, with an option to Retry sending. If this isn't the behavior you're seeing, it's a bug and I'd like to fix it. What version are you on and which OS?
  • By "already used", do you mean you already had it paired to your phone using Liam's app? I had only tested the auto battery curve with a fresh pair to PocketMesh, so it's possible it doesn't set the curve if the device was already paired in iOS/iPadOS. I will look into this. Thank you so much for the feedback. Let me know if you run into any other bugs, if anything else seems confusing, or have any feature requests!
Bullit 2026-02-01 6:45 p.m.
Hey Avion! Thank you very much for your reply! Okay now i see why its showing direct Makes sense! Is the auto reset path-option anywhere visualized? If yes i wasnt able to find it. But anyway its nice go be default on. But, unfortunatly, it wasnt showing how much attempts it already made. It was just stuck at „sending“ without further information how many attempts already done. Tried it again at this moment. It stays on „sending“ and shows no attempt counter nor flood approach or anything else. It goes straight from „sending“ to „failed“ after about 20 seconds. I‘m on iOS 26.1 and i am using an iPhone 12 Pro (i know its kinda old ) And yeah thats exactly what i meaned. The specific T1000E was already paired to Liams App. But if this is considered as normal behaviour i can live with that easily. I just wanted to mention it so you can check/say if this is as intended or a bug You‘re welcome! Happy to help with such a small effort on my side You‘re the one who deserves a „thank you“ for all the effort you‘re putting in!
1
Bullit
Hey Avion! Thank you very much for your reply! Okay now i see why its showing direct Makes sense! Is the auto reset path-option anywhere visualized? If yes i wasnt able to find it. But anyway its nice go be default on. But, unfortunatly, it wasnt showing how much attempts it already made. It was just stuck at „sending“ without further information how many attempts already done. Tried it again at this moment. It stays on „sending“ and shows no attempt counter nor flood approach or anything else. It goes straight from „sending“ to „failed“ after about 20 seconds. I‘m on iOS 26.1 and i am using an iPhone 12 Pro (i know its kinda old ) And yeah thats exactly what i meaned. The specific T1000E was already paired to Liams App. But if this is considered as normal behaviour i can live with that easily. I just wanted to mention it so you can check/say if this is as intended or a bug You‘re welcome! Happy to help with such a small effort on my side You‘re the one who deserves a „thank you“ for all the effort you‘re putting in!
avion 2026-02-01 6:48 p.m.
ah okay, then that is a regression. It should show the number of attempts in real time. What type of messages was it? Direct message?
Bullit 2026-02-01 6:52 p.m.
Yeah DM
avion 2026-02-01 6:52 p.m.
Thanks, I'll look into that!
Bullit 2026-02-01 6:53 p.m.
You‘re welcome! Thank YOU for your hard work man
1
avion 2026-02-01 7:37 p.m.
@Bullit After looking into it, that feature was "temporarily" removed a long time ago. I meant to put it back in, but looks like I forgot Thanks for the reminder, will try to get it into the next version.
Bullit 2026-02-01 8:28 p.m.
Happens to the best ones I mean its not a game breaker. Just nice to have Sometimes i was just wondering „did it even tried?“ , „did it already tried 5 times?“. But i‘m happy that i found a mistake and not myself being dumb
avion 2026-02-03 2:39 p.m.
0.6.0 has been submitted for review! Sorry for the long wait on this one.

What's Changed

Chat
  • Infinite scroll pagination for loading older messages
  • Improved message retry feedback showing attempt count
Discovery & Contacts
  • Node count displayed in search placeholder
  • Filtering, search, sorting, and clear functionality in the Discover page
  • Show all discovered nodes on discover page
v1.12 Firmware Support
  • Auto-add settings and node storage UI
  • Contact deletion protocol support
  • Backward compatibility for pre-v1.12 firmware
Advanced Settings
  • Updated Node settings to include toggles for firmware v1.12 features
Notifications
  • Tapping channel notifications now navigates directly to that channel
CLI
  • New CLI tool for repeater administration
  • ZSH style text suggestions and tab completion
  • Sessions allow you to manage multiple repeaters/rooms at once
Localization
  • Added some missing UI translations
  • Improved date formatting using Apple's localized formats
Other Improvements
  • Improved BLE state restoration handling
  • Better path display truncation for long hop lists in RX log

Bug Fixes

  • BLE: Resolved contact sync failures and race conditions (packet ordering, write pacing, continuation leaks)
  • Badge counts: Now correctly filtered by current device only (multi-device users saw wrong counts)
  • Messages: URLs within @mentions no longer incorrectly parsed as links or show link previews
  • Added repeater first byte collision handling. Helps with any screen that shows a list of repeaters. Selects repeaters in this order: Distance (if location services enabled) > Last advert time IPA is available for those who sideload: https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.6.0
 (edited)
3
3

Build 0.6.0 has been approved! Let me know if you run into any bugs. Enjoy!

Jeff [K1HFZ] 2026-02-07 11:11 a.m.
The share location publically option won't stay on... is this by default?
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-07 7:04 p.m.
I’ve been using a TCP connection to a wifi node at home over Tailscale, and it seems to drop the moment the app loses focus.
Jeff [K1HFZ]
The share location publically option won't stay on... is this by default?
avion 2026-02-07 8:15 p.m.
It’s a bug in 0.6.0, the toggle doesn’t persist correctly. It’s fixed in 0.7.0 which should be coming out early next week.
1
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
I’ve been using a TCP connection to a wifi node at home over Tailscale, and it seems to drop the moment the app loses focus.
avion 2026-02-07 8:16 p.m.
Unfortunately that’s a limitation with iOS. They don’t allow background TCP connections as far as I’m aware
8:17 p.m.
Not without special permission like VPNs
avion
Unfortunately that’s a limitation with iOS. They don’t allow background TCP connections as far as I’m aware
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-07 8:19 p.m.
That’s too bad. Any idea if they’d allow a persistent websockets connection? Might be a fun client mode.
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
That’s too bad. Any idea if they’d allow a persistent websockets connection? Might be a fun client mode.
avion 2026-02-07 8:23 p.m.
I don’t think so, but I will definitely double check. When I researched it, it seemed BLE was the only thing Apple allows to be semi-persistent in the background. The only way to wake up an app in the background is to send a push notification via Apple Push Notifications. The way that would work is to have a node connected to a small program on a computer that acts as a proxy and sends/receives notifications to a central server which would then send out an APN to your app which would allow it to wake up. But that would require me to spin up a server and has a bunch of privacy implications. (edited)
avion
I don’t think so, but I will definitely double check. When I researched it, it seemed BLE was the only thing Apple allows to be semi-persistent in the background. The only way to wake up an app in the background is to send a push notification via Apple Push Notifications. The way that would work is to have a node connected to a small program on a computer that acts as a proxy and sends/receives notifications to a central server which would then send out an APN to your app which would allow it to wake up. But that would require me to spin up a server and has a bunch of privacy implications. (edited)
[PNW] HerculesMulligan 2026-02-07 8:31 p.m.
Yeah, I think most people would opt out of that.
avion 2026-02-07 8:31 p.m.
I agree
8:33 p.m.
If I ever tried that, it would have to be E2EE and only send a blank notification to wake up the app which would then check on its own for the update. But that sounds like a large task that I’m not willing to commit to. Especially since implementing encryption requires complying with export laws
1
avion 2026-02-08 4:01 p.m.
0.7.0 has been submitted for review!

What's Changed

Notification Settings
  • New granular channel notification settings: All, Mentions Only, Mute. Access by tapping the "i" in the upper right of a channel. Not yet available in DMs.
Reactions
  • Send and receive reactions
  • In order to match the reactions to the correct message, a hash is created and sent along with the reaction. Example:
@[user] b45pc4ek This was done in an effort to be as friendly to other clients as possible.
  • Note: Reactions will only be rendered as a proper reaction if the other user is using PocketMesh or their client has implemented the same way PocketMesh is creating hashes. If you are a client developer and would like to add interoperability support, check out https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/blob/dev/docs/Reactions.md GPS Location Updates
  • Added support for both device and phone GPS location updates
  • Updates your node's location every time you send an advert Ping Repeater
  • You can now ping repeaters if they are 0 hop. Access the new button by selecting a repeater in the Nodes tab. New Message Divider
  • If you've received more than 10 new messages, you'll now see a "New Messages" divider CLI
  • Add new nodes and channel commands Trace Path Map Optimizations
  • No longer laggy if you have hundreds of repeaters on your map
  • No more delay when tapping repeaters to build a path
  • Repeaters don't disappear upon a successful trace, so you can add more repeaters after the trace Line of Sight Optimizations
  • Added repeater labels and clustering
  • No longer laggy if you have hundreds of repeaters on your map
  • Added loading states so the app doesn't seem frozen if the API call to Open-Meteo is slow ## Bug Fixes
  • Fix CLI text and toolbar buttons getting cut off when using Zoomed display and/or larger text
  • Fix CLI not resetting state when disconnecting/changing devices
  • Fix iPad not being able to start a DM with a contact
  • Fix Telemetry toggles not persisting
  • Fix Telemetry share with trusted contacts not functioning
  • Fix Radio Configuration settings disappearing when clearing Frequency or TX Power
  • Fix Map mode trace getting stuck if you had batch mode enabled in the List tab
  • Workaround for issue on Line of Sight and Trace Path maps where tapping a repeater then immediately panning would trigger a zoom. This was done by completely disabling the double-tap-to-zoom feature. Sideload IPA is available here: https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.7.0 Thank you for all the great bug reports and feedback
(edited)
2
1
1
2
1

Hey thanks for testing PocketMesh! If you're on 0.6.0, you won't be able to change that yet. It is coming out in 0.7.0 which just got submitted to Apple for TestFlight review. They usually approve it in about 1 business day.

exciting! Thank you

Apologies if this gets asked a lot, but is there a way to filter contacts so it shows only repeaters?
avion 2026-02-08 7:32 p.m.
No, not at the moment. You can filter it down to repeaters and rooms by tapping on "Network" in the Nodes tab, but not just repeaters yet. I'm still thinking about how best to implement the separation there.

avion
0.7.0 has been submitted for review!

What's Changed

Notification Settings
  • New granular channel notification settings: All, Mentions Only, Mute. Access by tapping the "i" in the upper right of a channel. Not yet available in DMs.
Reactions
  • Send and receive reactions
  • In order to match the reactions to the correct message, a hash is created and sent along with the reaction. Example:
@[user] b45pc4ek This was done in an effort to be as friendly to other clients as possible.
  • Note: Reactions will only be rendered as a proper reaction if the other user is using PocketMesh or their client has implemented the same way PocketMesh is creating hashes. If you are a client developer and would like to add interoperability support, check out https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/blob/dev/docs/Reactions.md GPS Location Updates
  • Added support for both device and phone GPS location updates
  • Updates your node's location every time you send an advert Ping Repeater
  • You can now ping repeaters if they are 0 hop. Access the new button by selecting a repeater in the Nodes tab. New Message Divider
  • If you've received more than 10 new messages, you'll now see a "New Messages" divider CLI
  • Add new nodes and channel commands Trace Path Map Optimizations
  • No longer laggy if you have hundreds of repeaters on your map
  • No more delay when tapping repeaters to build a path
  • Repeaters don't disappear upon a successful trace, so you can add more repeaters after the trace Line of Sight Optimizations
  • Added repeater labels and clustering
  • No longer laggy if you have hundreds of repeaters on your map
  • Added loading states so the app doesn't seem frozen if the API call to Open-Meteo is slow ## Bug Fixes
  • Fix CLI text and toolbar buttons getting cut off when using Zoomed display and/or larger text
  • Fix CLI not resetting state when disconnecting/changing devices
  • Fix iPad not being able to start a DM with a contact
  • Fix Telemetry toggles not persisting
  • Fix Telemetry share with trusted contacts not functioning
  • Fix Radio Configuration settings disappearing when clearing Frequency or TX Power
  • Fix Map mode trace getting stuck if you had batch mode enabled in the List tab
  • Workaround for issue on Line of Sight and Trace Path maps where tapping a repeater then immediately panning would trigger a zoom. This was done by completely disabling the double-tap-to-zoom feature. Sideload IPA is available here: https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.7.0 Thank you for all the great bug reports and feedback
(edited)

Great! I’ll give you a starter AI translation then you can create a PR with fixes. https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/blob/dev/TRANSLATIONS.md I’m not very familiar with CN, I’ve heard there are different ones, like traditional and simplified. Which one should I add? (edited)

avion 2026-02-11 3:32 p.m.
0.7.1 has been submitted for review!

What's Changed

  • Bluetooth Signal Strength Indicator: Now visible on the "Connect Device" screen to help you gauge connection quality at a glance.
  • Improved Device Visibility: Non-connectable Bluetooth devices are now grayed out, making it easier to spot available ones.
  • Faster Connections: Enhanced speed for connecting and reconnecting in specific scenarios.
  • Chinese Language Support: Full translation added for Chinese users.
  • UTF-8 Language Compatibility: Better handling for languages using UTF-8 characters.
  • Channel Support in Repeater Telemetry: Added telemetry channels support in the repeater status view. ## Bug Fixes
  • Resolved an issue where message notifications weren't fully suppressed during the final stages of syncing.
  • Fixed a problem causing queued messages to be permanently lost if the device disconnected mid-sync.
  • Shortened excessively long connection times for devices on firmware versions older than v1.12.
  • On iPad, eliminated the gap between the keyboard and input bar in chat views.
  • On iPad, corrected a rotation glitch that created large gaps in the chat interface.
  • Corrected the display of maximum node capacity in the Device Info section.
  • Prevented repeat counts from incorrectly increasing when using the "Send Again" feature.
  • Adjusted the long-press message action sheet to avoid overflowing at larger text sizes.
  • Fixed telemetry readings showing positive values instead of negative for voltage.
  • Added a period (.) key to the IP address entry keypad. Sideload IPA available on GitHub: https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.7.1
 (edited)
3
3
2
2
2
2
3
3
avion
0.7.1 has been submitted for review!

What's Changed

  • Bluetooth Signal Strength Indicator: Now visible on the "Connect Device" screen to help you gauge connection quality at a glance.
  • Improved Device Visibility: Non-connectable Bluetooth devices are now grayed out, making it easier to spot available ones.
  • Faster Connections: Enhanced speed for connecting and reconnecting in specific scenarios.
  • Chinese Language Support: Full translation added for Chinese users.
  • UTF-8 Language Compatibility: Better handling for languages using UTF-8 characters.
  • Channel Support in Repeater Telemetry: Added telemetry channels support in the repeater status view. ## Bug Fixes
  • Resolved an issue where message notifications weren't fully suppressed during the final stages of syncing.
  • Fixed a problem causing queued messages to be permanently lost if the device disconnected mid-sync.
  • Shortened excessively long connection times for devices on firmware versions older than v1.12.
  • On iPad, eliminated the gap between the keyboard and input bar in chat views.
  • On iPad, corrected a rotation glitch that created large gaps in the chat interface.
  • Corrected the display of maximum node capacity in the Device Info section.
  • Prevented repeat counts from incorrectly increasing when using the "Send Again" feature.
  • Adjusted the long-press message action sheet to avoid overflowing at larger text sizes.
  • Fixed telemetry readings showing positive values instead of negative for voltage.
  • Added a period (.) key to the IP address entry keypad. Sideload IPA available on GitHub: https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.7.1
 (edited)
romeooscarbravo 2026-02-13 10:36 a.m.
It seems the period is not for every county fixed? Dutch over here still seeing the same , instead of the . For IP settings. Could it still be a bug?
romeooscarbravo
It seems the period is not for every county fixed? Dutch over here still seeing the same , instead of the . For IP settings. Could it still be a bug?
avion 2026-02-13 11:23 a.m.
hm... no, it should be a period for all languages. It worked in the simulator when I was testing, but I must have missed something. I'll take a look!
avion 2026-02-13 12:01 p.m.
ahh I figured it out. I didn't realize I had to switch the region, not the language. So I thought my original fix worked, but I was still using the US number pad with the period It doesn't look like I can switch it to be a period visually, but I can replace the commas with periods. Will be fixed in the next release!
1
Eddy 2026-02-14 5:30 a.m.
Is there a way to block a sender from a channel? So the sender is not in the contact list (and just spamming in a channel with a bot)? How to block that one sender? (edited)
romeooscarbravo 2026-02-14 7:21 a.m.
Don’t think that is possible. Which channel is the bot in?
Eddy 2026-02-14 7:35 a.m.
In the test channel. I don’t mean a block only in one channel. But a total block of that user. I can block a user that is in my contact list. But I don’t know how to block a user that isn’t in my contact list. I hoped to use a click on the user and be able to block the user.
Eddy
In the test channel. I don’t mean a block only in one channel. But a total block of that user. I can block a user that is in my contact list. But I don’t know how to block a user that isn’t in my contact list. I hoped to use a click on the user and be able to block the user.
avion 2026-02-14 11:13 a.m.
That isn’t possible yet, but will be soon. I’m adding a way to do that in the next release, along with the option to block key words. But it will be somewhat limited due to the way channels work in MeshCore. We can add a sender name to the block list, but since we don’t receive their key in channels, it will have to be matched by their sender name only which comes with two caveats: 1) The sender can easily change their sender name. 2) If another user has the same name they will also be blocked. That being said, I think adding the “Block Sender” feature’s benefits out weigh the downsides. (edited)
11:14 a.m.
Thank you for bringing that up!
Eddy 2026-02-14 11:15 a.m.
Thanks @avion btw the app works great.
1
avion 2026-02-14 11:17 a.m.
Happy to hear that! Let me know if you run into any bugs or have other feature requests. Always happy to hear them
1
avion
Happy to hear that! Let me know if you run into any bugs or have other feature requests. Always happy to hear them
WZNRXSN 2026-02-15 11:28 p.m.
Hi,I spent some time using PocketMesh 1. I noticed it hasn’t been fixed.Meanwhile.,The spacing seems to have gotten larger. 2.this area is hidden from view. 3.Even when no contacts are shown on the map, users should still be able to view it — especially in the wilderness, where knowing your own location is critical. 4.The “Zero-hop Broadcast” button should be visible on the main/chat screen instead of being hidden inside the Device Connection page — it’s currently inconvenient to access. Feature Request: 1.It would be helpful to have built-in tools on the map page to measure both linear distance (e.g., between two points) and area (e.g., polygon selection). 2.The chat interface should support sending and rendering coordinate-type messages (e.g., latitude/longitude). Rendered coordinates must be clickable and trigger an embedded map control or native map view to show the exact location.The developers of Meshcore Open are also working on this feature. I have submitted a feature request to them as well, so we can discuss and unify the message format for coordinates. 3.Display the latest radio communication metrics (e.g., SNR, RSSI, frequency, modulation mode, etc.) on the contact’s detail page. This data should reflect the most recent successful transmission from that contact. 4.Implement a ranging measurement tool that periodically broadcasts data packets to one person. (edited)
WZNRXSN
Hi,I spent some time using PocketMesh 1. I noticed it hasn’t been fixed.Meanwhile.,The spacing seems to have gotten larger. 2.this area is hidden from view. 3.Even when no contacts are shown on the map, users should still be able to view it — especially in the wilderness, where knowing your own location is critical. 4.The “Zero-hop Broadcast” button should be visible on the main/chat screen instead of being hidden inside the Device Connection page — it’s currently inconvenient to access. Feature Request: 1.It would be helpful to have built-in tools on the map page to measure both linear distance (e.g., between two points) and area (e.g., polygon selection). 2.The chat interface should support sending and rendering coordinate-type messages (e.g., latitude/longitude). Rendered coordinates must be clickable and trigger an embedded map control or native map view to show the exact location.The developers of Meshcore Open are also working on this feature. I have submitted a feature request to them as well, so we can discuss and unify the message format for coordinates. 3.Display the latest radio communication metrics (e.g., SNR, RSSI, frequency, modulation mode, etc.) on the contact’s detail page. This data should reflect the most recent successful transmission from that contact. 4.Implement a ranging measurement tool that periodically broadcasts data packets to one person. (edited)
avion 2026-02-15 11:49 p.m.
Hi, thank you for the detailed feedback! 1. This is fixed in 0.8.0 which will hopefully be approved tomorrow 2. This should be fixed in 0.8.0 as well, but let me know if it isn't 3. I've been meaning to do this, I'll get around to it soon. But please note that internet is required to use the map. Offline map is not a feature at this point in time. 4. Unfortunately I don't think I can move that button out of it's current menu. On iPhone, there is no space in the toolbar for it. Re: Feature Requests 1. If I'm understanding you correctly, you can do this with the Line of Sight tool in the Tools tab. 2. This is a good idea, I'll keep it in mind 3. I believe it would only be possible to obtain this information in Direct Messages. We do not have a way in current MeshCore firmware to tell for sure who is sending a message in a channel, so we cannot link the RX data reliably. Because of this limitation, I will likely not add this feature. But please let me know if you disagree and why. 4. Could you explain what you mean by "ranging measurement tool"? Do you mean to simply send a message to a contact periodically and log whether or not it was received?
avion
Hi, thank you for the detailed feedback! 1. This is fixed in 0.8.0 which will hopefully be approved tomorrow 2. This should be fixed in 0.8.0 as well, but let me know if it isn't 3. I've been meaning to do this, I'll get around to it soon. But please note that internet is required to use the map. Offline map is not a feature at this point in time. 4. Unfortunately I don't think I can move that button out of it's current menu. On iPhone, there is no space in the toolbar for it. Re: Feature Requests 1. If I'm understanding you correctly, you can do this with the Line of Sight tool in the Tools tab. 2. This is a good idea, I'll keep it in mind 3. I believe it would only be possible to obtain this information in Direct Messages. We do not have a way in current MeshCore firmware to tell for sure who is sending a message in a channel, so we cannot link the RX data reliably. Because of this limitation, I will likely not add this feature. But please let me know if you disagree and why. 4. Could you explain what you mean by "ranging measurement tool"? Do you mean to simply send a message to a contact periodically and log whether or not it was received?
WZNRXSN 2026-02-15 11:51 p.m.
Could you explain what you mean by "ranging measurement tool"? Do you mean to simply send a message to a contact periodically and log whether or not it was received? Yes
1
11:51 p.m.
Thanks
WZNRXSN
Could you explain what you mean by "ranging measurement tool"? Do you mean to simply send a message to a contact periodically and log whether or not it was received? Yes
avion 2026-02-15 11:52 p.m.
Let me think about that one. A core idea of MeshCore is to limit the amount of packets that are sent out, so I'd like to keep PocketMesh as quiet as possible. But I do see the benefit of a tool like that. Like for finding dead spots or the best place for a new repeater. I will think about it.
avion
Let me think about that one. A core idea of MeshCore is to limit the amount of packets that are sent out, so I'd like to keep PocketMesh as quiet as possible. But I do see the benefit of a tool like that. Like for finding dead spots or the best place for a new repeater. I will think about it.
WZNRXSN 2026-02-15 11:54 p.m.
It's used to test the point-to-point connection status, just for testing purposes. Thank you for taking the time to look at my suggestion.
WZNRXSN
It's used to test the point-to-point connection status, just for testing purposes. Thank you for taking the time to look at my suggestion.
avion 2026-02-15 11:57 p.m.
Of course! I'm always happy to receive feedback. Thank you for taking the time to write that up and annotate the screenshots.

12:02 a.m.

WZNRXSN 2026-02-16 12:04 a.m.
Because when i changes the radio settings, it takes some time to communicate normally after restarting. (edited)
avion 2026-02-16 12:05 a.m.
Your device changes the settings automatically? Or you change in PocketMesh settings?
WZNRXSN
Because when i changes the radio settings, it takes some time to communicate normally after restarting. (edited)
WZNRXSN 2026-02-16 12:06 a.m.
I often use the Zero-Hop button to manually find the other party. (edited)
WZNRXSN
I often use the Zero-Hop button to manually find the other party. (edited)
avion 2026-02-16 12:12 a.m.
Sorry, I'm not understanding. Do you mean that you have the automatic update location setting on, and you're using the zero-hop advert to update your location to the other party? (edited)
avion
Sorry, I'm not understanding. Do you mean that you have the automatic update location setting on, and you're using the zero-hop advert to update your location to the other party? (edited)
WZNRXSN 2026-02-16 12:15 a.m.
After I changed the LoRa radio settings, even though the two devices had communicated before, it takes a long time to successfully communicate after the modification. However, I can connect quickly using the Zero-Hop button.
avion 2026-02-16 12:17 a.m.
I think I understand. Would an "Advert" button near the radio change settings be helpful?
avion
I think I understand. Would an "Advert" button near the radio change settings be helpful?
WZNRXSN 2026-02-16 12:18 a.m.
Good idea
2
avion 2026-02-16 12:19 a.m.
Let me think about it. You can already advert by tapping the radio icon in the settings page, but it is two taps instead of one. But adding the advert buttons to the Radio section would make it quite busy with the new Repeat Mode toggle. But I understand your pain point.
avion
Let me think about it. You can already advert by tapping the radio icon in the settings page, but it is two taps instead of one. But adding the advert buttons to the Radio section would make it quite busy with the new Repeat Mode toggle. But I understand your pain point.
WZNRXSN 2026-02-16 12:20 a.m.
Can it automatically broadcast a few times after changing the radio settings?
avion 2026-02-16 12:21 a.m.
That is what came to mind, yeah. I could make it an Advanced Setting.
1
avion 2026-02-16 1:31 a.m.
0.8.0 has been submitted to TF for approval!

What's Changed

  • Inline Images & GIFs: When you receive a link to a gif or image, see it in-line instead of having to tap the link. Also supports the meshcore-open gif format. But please note that I have no plans to include a gif picker to send gifs at this time.
  • Channel Sender Blocking: Block senders within channels by long pressing on their message. Manage blocked senders and contacts in Settings.
  • Telemetry Status History: See a history of your repeater's telemetry. (Only saves new data when loaded at least 15 minutes apart.)
  • Repeater Receive Errors: Support for showing packet receive errors in repeater telemetry status view.
  • Telemetry Grouping: Channel grouping in repeater telemetry status view.
  • Remove Non-Favorite Nodes: New Remove non-favorite nodes button found in Settings > Advanced Settings.
  • Companion Config Export/Import: Export and import node configuration. Found in Settings > Advanced Settings. Compatible with Liam's MeshCore app.
  • Companion Repeat Mode: Support for the new Repeat Mode. Your companion must be on firmware >= v1.13.0
  • Bluetooth Signal Strength: See bluetooth signal strength to your saved nodes in the Connect sheet.
  • Chinese Translation Corrections: Thank you @WZNRXSN ## Bug Fixes
  • Fix inability to enter periods in WiFi node connection sheets for locales that use comma instead of period.
  • Fix device not being removed from system Bluetooth when factory resetting companion device
  • Fix iPad gap between keyboard and input field
3
2
2
1
1
1
2
avion
0.8.0 has been submitted to TF for approval!

What's Changed

  • Inline Images & GIFs: When you receive a link to a gif or image, see it in-line instead of having to tap the link. Also supports the meshcore-open gif format. But please note that I have no plans to include a gif picker to send gifs at this time.
  • Channel Sender Blocking: Block senders within channels by long pressing on their message. Manage blocked senders and contacts in Settings.
  • Telemetry Status History: See a history of your repeater's telemetry. (Only saves new data when loaded at least 15 minutes apart.)
  • Repeater Receive Errors: Support for showing packet receive errors in repeater telemetry status view.
  • Telemetry Grouping: Channel grouping in repeater telemetry status view.
  • Remove Non-Favorite Nodes: New Remove non-favorite nodes button found in Settings > Advanced Settings.
  • Companion Config Export/Import: Export and import node configuration. Found in Settings > Advanced Settings. Compatible with Liam's MeshCore app.
  • Companion Repeat Mode: Support for the new Repeat Mode. Your companion must be on firmware >= v1.13.0
  • Bluetooth Signal Strength: See bluetooth signal strength to your saved nodes in the Connect sheet.
  • Chinese Translation Corrections: Thank you @WZNRXSN ## Bug Fixes
  • Fix inability to enter periods in WiFi node connection sheets for locales that use comma instead of period.
  • Fix device not being removed from system Bluetooth when factory resetting companion device
  • Fix iPad gap between keyboard and input field

avion
0.8.0 has been submitted to TF for approval!

What's Changed

  • Inline Images & GIFs: When you receive a link to a gif or image, see it in-line instead of having to tap the link. Also supports the meshcore-open gif format. But please note that I have no plans to include a gif picker to send gifs at this time.
  • Channel Sender Blocking: Block senders within channels by long pressing on their message. Manage blocked senders and contacts in Settings.
  • Telemetry Status History: See a history of your repeater's telemetry. (Only saves new data when loaded at least 15 minutes apart.)
  • Repeater Receive Errors: Support for showing packet receive errors in repeater telemetry status view.
  • Telemetry Grouping: Channel grouping in repeater telemetry status view.
  • Remove Non-Favorite Nodes: New Remove non-favorite nodes button found in Settings > Advanced Settings.
  • Companion Config Export/Import: Export and import node configuration. Found in Settings > Advanced Settings. Compatible with Liam's MeshCore app.
  • Companion Repeat Mode: Support for the new Repeat Mode. Your companion must be on firmware >= v1.13.0
  • Bluetooth Signal Strength: See bluetooth signal strength to your saved nodes in the Connect sheet.
  • Chinese Translation Corrections: Thank you @WZNRXSN ## Bug Fixes
  • Fix inability to enter periods in WiFi node connection sheets for locales that use comma instead of period.
  • Fix device not being removed from system Bluetooth when factory resetting companion device
  • Fix iPad gap between keyboard and input field
megadimich 2026-02-17 12:31 p.m.
Thanks for the app. It keeps getting better and better! You guys are awesome!!!
2
Jeff [K1HFZ] 2026-02-17 5:23 p.m.
For the 1w rak 3401, will they allow 30 dbm on the tx power… seems stuck at 22…
5:24 p.m.
Or is that more of a firmware issue/coded…
Uncle Lit
Just downloaded the app from test flight. First thing came to mind is I don't think you need to say your app is unofficial. Your app is just as official as any app that works with meshcore.
avion 2026-02-17 5:41 p.m.
Thank you, I'm planning on changing that as well as the name of the app in the future. When I first started the project, I thought MeshCore was sort of like Meshtastic and didn't want to get into any naming disputes.
1
Jeff [K1HFZ]
For the 1w rak 3401, will they allow 30 dbm on the tx power… seems stuck at 22…
avion 2026-02-17 5:43 p.m.
PocketMesh reads MAX_LORA_TX_POWER from the firmware. It looks like the rak3401 doesn't have that set, so it will default to LORA_TX_POWER which is set to 22 Are you able to set it to 30 in Liam's app? If so, it's something I misunderstood about the firmware.
Jeff [K1HFZ] 2026-02-17 5:44 p.m.
No it doesn’t take it there either
5:44 p.m.
Should be able to go to 30
avion 2026-02-17 5:44 p.m.
Got it, then I would open an issue on the firmware's GitHub for that.
Jeff [K1HFZ] 2026-02-17 5:46 p.m.
Ya I’m guessing though st 22 we may be fine since the amp boosts it beyond 22, so it may not really matter and could do more harm than good.. thank you!
1
Jeff [K1HFZ]
Ya I’m guessing though st 22 we may be fine since the amp boosts it beyond 22, so it may not really matter and could do more harm than good.. thank you!
🗿 2026-02-17 8:36 p.m.
I’m pretty sure all Lora radios are 22 max and the LNA pushes it up to max. There was an interesting thread about the v4 LNA I can try and forward but two separate people tested and at 20 it hits max and saturates the LNA it may be different for the 1w but it’s possibly worth testing what your max output before saturation is as I was able to bring mine down from 22 to 20 with no noticeable range reduction and a noticeable reduction of battery usage
Jeff [K1HFZ] 2026-02-17 8:37 p.m.
Good points... thank you...
avion
Got it, then I would open an issue on the firmware's GitHub for that.
WZNRXSN 2026-02-17 9:20 p.m.
Hi avion，I would like to ask when the app will be released on the Apple Store.
WZNRXSN
Hi avion，I would like to ask when the app will be released on the Apple Store.
avion 2026-02-17 9:31 p.m.
Rough estimate is a couple months, but no promises. Once I get all the features I want in it, I want to let it sit on TestFlight for a month or two to get as many bug reports as possible before putting it on the App Store.

romeooscarbravo 2026-02-19 12:27 a.m.
@avion Agree, you/team focused a lot on features in the past amount of time. I think the shift to user interface which could be further optimized with community feedback. Maybe you can do a shout-out for UI feedback? At my iPhone 17 Pro, the readability of this app is not as great as the official MeshCore app, and yeah, I'm not a fan of that official app because of the annoying red and green banners. But the chat windows are better to read in the official MeshCore app than in PocketMesh, we can't let that happen . My first feedback: -Can't put my finger on it, but I have spoken before about the font size—that has increased a bit, right? -Maybe the usernames (the colored ones) should be reduced in size, which makes the real text (what the user typed) attract more attention from your eyes. -There is *a lot *of unused space in the chat windows, a lot of black background which could be used for readbility. Please make the text balloons wider, which could improve the overall readability."

1

Please make the text balloons wider, which could improve the overall readability. Agree

avion 2026-02-19 12:31 a.m.
Thank you for that detailed feedback, I have been meaning to ask the users about the chat view. The interesting thing about my area is that I have hardly any MeshCore activity, so I almost never use the actual chat portion I think all of those are a great idea, but I'm unsure about the usernames. I initially made those larger because of feedback that they were too small. But perhaps I went overboard? And yes, the font size increased a little. It also adjusts with the phone's accessibility settings.

WZNRXSN 2026-02-19 12:38 a.m.
I agree with your opinion. I think ensuring basic functionality and reasonable page layout are high priorities. The Meshastic Android app is a complete mess right now.
romeooscarbravo 2026-02-19 12:38 a.m.
romeooscarbravo
Let me first share some screenshots of a busy MeshCore network for your reference. When I looked again the “quoted” username is annoying me the most. It’s to big and to fat.
avion 2026-02-19 12:39 a.m.
I see. The color of the mentioned names are enough to distinguish it so they don't need to be bold. That makes sense. It looks like I can decrease the blank space between messages as well.
romeooscarbravo 2026-02-19 12:39 a.m.
And the normal MeshCore app to check out the balloon positioning, I suppose they used a fixed width which provides a more relaxing view.
romeooscarbravo
And the normal MeshCore app to check out the balloon positioning, I suppose they used a fixed width which provides a more relaxing view.
avion 2026-02-19 12:40 a.m.
I don't think it's quite fixed width, but I do see what you're saying. The text wrapping in PocketMesh is too agressive.
romeooscarbravo 2026-02-19 12:44 a.m.
And i love the path info underneath its small enough and im able to switch that off when I wanted. Yeah good catch, the wrapping is the correct optimization which makes it a bit "busy" at the screen. If the wrapping is less aggressive, the unused screenspace (the black parts) does fill-up better with text. So thats a good item to optimize. Feedback meant as positive to improve the app as community.
romeooscarbravo
And i love the path info underneath its small enough and im able to switch that off when I wanted. Yeah good catch, the wrapping is the correct optimization which makes it a bit "busy" at the screen. romeooscarbravo 2026-02-19 1:09 a.m.
So the developer is able to join a busy, maybe the bussiest mesh(?) of the EU at the moment remotely. Which helps him to optimize the app for real usage.
romeooscarbravo
So the developer is able to join a busy, maybe the bussiest mesh(?) of the EU at the moment remotely. Which helps him to optimize the app for real usage.
WZNRXSN 2026-02-19 1:11 a.m.
Are you saying using WiFi to remotely connect to the device, providing a network to connect?
romeooscarbravo 2026-02-19 1:15 a.m.
Yeah i could just open op a port on my router, secured to his source IP. And then he is able to use my companion and jumps into the 2700devices mesh network. A companion only allows one TCP connection. The only thing: the official meshcore app got an issue when the AP is not opened in main view, the TCP connection disconnects instandly. So you keep connecting every time, by pressing the buttons. Just a tip in advance before the setup is ready @avion some TCP heartbeat within PocketMesh could help your experience. I did not connect my wifi companion to PocketMesh at the moment, it running as a bot right now. (edited)
1
romeooscarbravo
Yeah i could just open op a port on my router, secured to his source IP. And then he is able to use my companion and jumps into the 2700devices mesh network. A companion only allows one TCP connection. The only thing: the official meshcore app got an issue when the AP is not opened in main view, the TCP connection disconnects instandly. So you keep connecting every time, by pressing the buttons. Just a tip in advance before the setup is ready @avion some TCP heartbeat within PocketMesh could help your experience. I did not connect my wifi companion to PocketMesh at the moment, it running as a bot right now. (edited)
avion 2026-02-19 1:16 a.m.
That is unfortunately an Apple limitation. They don't allow TCP connections in the background unless you get special permission for apps like VPNs
1:17 a.m.
But in my limited testing with wifi nodes, it stays connected in PocketMesh as long as I have the app open and screen on
avion
That is unfortunately an Apple limitation. They don't allow TCP connections in the background unless you get special permission for apps like VPNs
romeooscarbravo 2026-02-19 1:17 a.m.
Yeah i was wondering how WhatsApp does such implementations or Telegram. They use some auto-reconnect then?
avion 2026-02-19 1:18 a.m.
They use Apple Push Notifications (APN). Apple allows the app to wake up briefly if a push notification is sent from a server. (edited)
1:22 a.m.
I have toyed with the idea of building a bridge for wifi nodes that would send a push notification through a zero knowledge cloudflare worker proxy so that you could use PocketMesh without having a node on you. You would lose out on message contents though since the APN proxy would be zero knowledge. Notification would just be something like, "You have a new message". Then when PocketMesh gets the push notification from the proxy server, it can wake up briefly to connect to your computer running the bridge via Tailscale (or something similar), sync from the serial companion, then save the data and shutdown the connection during the small window Apple allows. But that is for after PocketMesh hits 1.0 if ever (edited)

Wolfgang 2026-02-22 11:20 a.m.
Feature Request: allow to select the ui language manually. I would like to use PocketMesh in English on a German iPad
Wolfgang
Feature Request: allow to select the ui language manually. I would like to use PocketMesh in English on a German iPad
avion 2026-02-22 11:50 a.m.
You can switch languages by doing the following:
  • Navigate to iOS/iPadOS System Settings
  • Search for PocketMesh
  • Change Preferred Language But it would be nice to have it in PocketMesh itself. I’ll look to see if it’s possible!

avion 2026-02-23 12:05 p.m.
0.9.0 has been submitted for review! It usually takes about a business day for the review to complete.

What's Changed

  • Node Discovery: New tool to scan for nearby repeaters and sensors. Find it in Tools.
  • Jump to Unread: New floating button in channels that scrolls you back to where you left off.
  • Settings redesigned: Reorganized into sub-pages (Radio, Location, Connection, Chat) for easier navigation.
  • Stale Node Cleanup: Automatically removes nodes older than a configurable time period after connecting to your radio. Toggle and configure in Settings > Advanced.
  • Device Reboot: Reboot your radio directly from Settings > Advanced.
  • Telemetry charts: Battery/voltage graphs now use the repeater's Battery Curve to set the Y-axis range, with a 500 mV buffer so charging at 100% stays visible.
  • Chat reply format: Quoted text is now more visually distinct. A proper reply bubble is planned.
  • Message actions sheet: Removed Liquid Glass transparency for better readability. Path hops are more compact so you can see more at a glance.
  • Chat UI: Tighter spacing between messages, wider bubbles, mentions styled with underline instead of bold.
  • RX Log: Entries now show the date alongside the timestamp.
  • BLE device support: Added default name prefixes for nRF52 and WisCore boards. ### Bug Fixes
  • Fixed keyboard gap on iPadOS 18.
  • Enlarged the message send button on iOS/iPadOS 18.
  • Tools on iPad now close when the radio disconnects to prevent stale data.
  • Added missing localizations for Low Battery notifications.
  • Fixed Settings buttons on iPad not appearing blue.
  • Fixed "Connected elsewhere" showing incorrectly when the radio was not connected elsewhere.
  • Fixed repeater name sometimes not populating in the Repeater Admin sheet.
  • Chinese translation fixes (thanks MGJ520). Unsigned sideload IPAs available at https://github.com/Avi0n/PocketMesh/releases/tag/v0.9.0
3

Zaos 2026-02-24 11:13 a.m.
Heyo
11:15 a.m.
I can submit an official request in the #issues tab but I was wondering if it would be possible to add a "Add custom button" to repeater management, almost like the ping button but you click this new "Add button" It will let you name the button, and then tie a command to that button press, So now that button is in your UI and next time you hit it, it runs that command, itll be super useful for people running custom stuff
11:16 a.m.
Like I personally have a car starter tied to meshcore, Currently its a client and I just text the client "Car start" but it would be so much easier if I could forego the client, and just have the car relay that I got, and have it run the command that I configure in firmware with just a button press
1
Andrew L - Central Texas Mesh 2026-02-24 11:25 a.m.
@avion Howdy! I just recently installed the PocketMesh app, and I am loving it so far! Thanks for all of your hard work! I have one request: Any chance that you could add the ability to use room servers when setting a manual path, or running trace routes? Room servers can also duel as repeaters, but at this point you can’t set a manual path using them inside your app. (It can discover them in a path, you just can’t manually set a path, or run trace routes.) Thank you!
Zaos
I can submit an official request in the #issues tab but I was wondering if it would be possible to add a "Add custom button" to repeater management, almost like the ping button but you click this new "Add button" It will let you name the button, and then tie a command to that button press, So now that button is in your UI and next time you hit it, it runs that command, itll be super useful for people running custom stuff
avion 2026-02-24 3:22 p.m.
H! Could you write up an issue for this? Include as many technical details as possible such as how you're modifying the firmware, what you expect to be able to send as part of the custom command, etc. I probably won't add a button in the Node Info page. But what I may do instead, is add support for custom text macros. So for example, you could send "car start" by starting to type "/c" then a list similar to the user mention list would appear above the input bar. Tapping "/car_start" would automatically send the preconfigured "car start" as a regular text message. The slash macros would be configured in Advanced Settings.
Andrew L - Central Texas Mesh
@avion Howdy! I just recently installed the PocketMesh app, and I am loving it so far! Thanks for all of your hard work! I have one request: Any chance that you could add the ability to use room servers when setting a manual path, or running trace routes? Room servers can also duel as repeaters, but at this point you can’t set a manual path using them inside your app. (It can discover them in a path, you just can’t manually set a path, or run trace routes.) Thank you!
avion 2026-02-24 3:24 p.m.
Howdy! I forgot rooms could act as repeaters... I wonder if I can tell whether a room is a repeater or not I'll look into it, thanks for the feedback and request!
Andrew L - Central Texas Mesh 2026-02-24 4:55 p.m.
Thank you! Yeah, I’m not sure if there’s a way to tell remotely if repeat is set on. It probably isn’t super common, but I figured it was worth noting. When you manually set a path in the original MC app it filters it by repeaters only by default, but you can add room servers if you wish to…
avion
Howdy! I forgot rooms could act as repeaters... I wonder if I can tell whether a room is a repeater or not I'll look into it, thanks for the feedback and request!
Andrew L - Central Texas Mesh 2026-02-24 6:36 p.m.
One other small thing: Not a big deal, but when you send a message, the app intermittently leaves the text in the text box even after you sent it. Doesn’t happen every time, but pretty commonly…
Andrew L - Central Texas Mesh
One other small thing: Not a big deal, but when you send a message, the app intermittently leaves the text in the text box even after you sent it. Doesn’t happen every time, but pretty commonly…
avion 2026-02-24 6:59 p.m.
Not a big deal
Please let me know even the little things! I like referring to these small annoyances as paper cuts. Fine if it's only one, but if they add up then it gets super frustrating to use the app. And I don't want that. So thank you for reporting this, I'll get it fixed in the next release.
1
avion
Not a big deal
Please let me know even the little things! I like referring to these small annoyances as paper cuts. Fine if it's only one, but if they add up then it gets super frustrating to use the app. And I don't want that. So thank you for reporting this, I'll get it fixed in the next release.

avion 2026-02-25 4:19 p.m.
The idea is to make the app user friendly for those just getting into MeshCore and who may not understand what Frequency, Bandwidth, Spreading Factor, etc. are or what to set them to. Presets are an easy way to help onboard new users.

crisprCAS9
a minor usability nitpick… i would love it if the connect button went away on this dialog. quicker to just tap the node i want to connect to
avion 2026-02-25 4:20 p.m.
I needed the Connect button because it fired a check to ensure the ble device wasn't connected to another app. But now that I do an RSSI check, I can do it there instead. Will have this out in the next release!

avion 2026-03-01 6:20 p.m.
It would be cool to support non-Apple platforms, but it would require an almost complete rewrite so it's probably not something that will ever happen. I wanted a fully native feeling app for Apple devices, so decided on Swift instead of cross-platform Flutter.

2.And the location data broadcast by other devices cannot be updated on the map in a timely manner (edited)
avion 2026-03-03 11:28 a.m.
If you're on the Map tab and other devices update their location, you can press the Refresh button in the upper right. Updating node locations live is a cool idea, but it sounds somewhat complicated to add. I'm planning on switching to MapLibre for all the maps so that I can eventually support offline maps (can't do this with Apple Maps API unfortunately), so I may look at adding this feature at the same time. (edited)
WZNRXSN
This also brings up a relatively practical feature: an emoji image hosting service, where instead of relying on fixed image hosts like in the Meshcore Open app, you can directly use trusted URLs for GIFs, PNGs, and more.
avion 2026-03-03 11:30 a.m.
I like the idea, but it would require me to pay for a server to run. Maybe once I add IAPs I can add this as a paid feature to help with server and development costs. I do think it would be fun to have gifs and stickers in the app.
WZNRXSN
4.Another problem is that cannot quote your own messages in the channel. (edited)
avion 2026-03-03 11:30 a.m.
This will be added when I get around to adding the Reply bubble UI

avion 2026-03-03 11:32 a.m.
This goes along with the MapLibre update, but I'll probably make this static instead of scrollable. Will also probably change the "Open in Maps" button to open the node in the in-app Maps tab instead of the system Map app. I think that's what people would expect (for any lurkers, let me know if I'm wrong).

megadimich

avion 2026-03-03 1:45 p.m.
I don't plan on changing the way PocketMesh sends reactions, I'm not a fan of how meshcore open sends text that others can't read.
