avion 2026-03-03 8:46 p.m.
0.10.0 has been submitted for review! It takes about a full business day to pass review.

What's Changed

  • Connect Device: One less tap to connect—simply tap on your device. Long press nodes to edit or remove them.
  • Live Activity: View your node’s connection status, packets per minute, and battery level at a glance. Enabled by default (disable in Settings).
  • Settings: Added direct link to system Settings to change the app language.
  • Advanced Settings: Import a pre-generated private key or generate your own. Optionally generate keys with a specific prefix.
  • Chat: @mention suggestions now sorted by most recent sender instead of alphabetically.
  • Chat: Detect malicious links and warn the user
  • Chat: Send button now has a 1-second cooldown after sending a message.
  • Chat: On iPad, the sheet that opens when long pressing on a message is now taller by default
  • Chat List: Added persistent filter tabs at the top of the Chats list and removed the filter button.
  • Node Avatars: Now shows emoji if name contains one.
  • Repeater Admin: Added “Contact Info” section.
  • Message Paths: Path names in long-press menus now resolve discovered node names when no contact exists.
  • Trace Path Tool: New “include discovered” toggle to select from nearby discovered nodes.
  • Trace Path Tool: Rooms now appear in the trace path list.
  • Discover: Discovered node rows now show the node ID hash for easier identification.
  • Node Discovery: Add discovered repeaters right from the tool. No need to go to Discovery to add. ### Bug Fixes
  • Fixed text input not clearing occasionally after sending a message.
  • Fixed crash in LoS tool when nodes are too spread out.
  • Removed accidental ability to chat with Room nodes.
  • Fixed Radio Button visual glitch on iPadOS 26.
  • Fix rooms saying "Connected" when the radio was manually disconnected
  • Centered emoji row in message long press sheet
  • Fix T1000-E not matching to OCV preset
4
1
1
2
1
1
1
2

8:55 p.m.
https://github.com/andyshinn/PocketMesh/commit/fdcfee05c7133e54f7c854e45e09dd78f525a87f

8:55 p.m.
enjoy a million macros!
andyshinn
no, just regular macOS. working BLE. but there are bugs. was done with Claude so there are rough edges. but the basics do work
avion 2026-03-04 8:55 p.m.
Ah okay that was my next question. I did explore a macOS version with catalyst a while ago, but found that some portions of the app didn't work, or caused crashes. So decided to shelve it until later to focus on iOS/iPadOS
andyshinn 2026-03-04 8:57 p.m.
ok. i didn't know which direction to go i just picked native macOS. no real rhyme or reason to try one way or the other. it seems there are some basic differences between macOS and iOS that don't seamlessly come over (like maybe UIKit is a iOS only thing?)
8:57 p.m.
i wonder if there is a UI style that is better supported between both without needing conditionals everywhere
avion 2026-03-04 8:59 p.m.
Yeah, that should be catalyst. Checking mac uses catalyst in the background to get the iPad app running on mac. As part of that though, you're limited in the APIs you can use. https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/mac-catalyst (edited)
Mac Catalyst | Apple Developer Documentation
Create a version of your iPad app that users can run on a Mac device.
9:04 p.m.
But I want it to feel like a real macOS app, not an iPad app running on macOS. So it might take a while. (edited)
andyshinn 2026-03-04 9:34 p.m.
that wouldn't be too bad. some iPad on macOS ones usually don't do a good job of making the UI work on macOS. but that screenshot looks good and probably easier to reason about code-wise

avion
Thanks! Let me know if you run into any bugs or have any feature requests (edited)
DeKo xD 2026-03-06 4:21 a.m.
Impressively, not many bugs so far (less than the official app). Maybe pinging multi-hop, idk if it’s because of the intermittency to my closed repeater, or if it’s direct pinging (if it is, a label “Direct Ping” would be more appropriate). I’ve totally replaced the official MeshCore app with this one. For features, here are some:
  • iCloud sync for telemetry history, passwords, battery curves
  • (if possible) Room Server management and telemetry
  • (I wouldn’t have much use for it, but some people would love) Apple shortcuts
  • a (simple) Apple Watch app. I know it wouldn’t work in background, but it would be good to be able to check and send messages without a phone
  • (I think) double ack settings is missing
 (edited)

avion
Hmm.. I haven't run into that issue yet with adding from the discover page. When you notice them disappear again, could you DM me your logs? It sounds like a sync bug, but would like to see if the logs tell a different story.
Eddy 2026-03-09 1:42 p.m.
Todays all the contacts again disapeared. I have send you a DM with the debug log. Hope you are able to find the problem. (edited)
Eddy
Todays all the contacts again disapeared. I have send you a DM with the debug log. Hope you are able to find the problem. (edited)
avion 2026-03-09 2:39 p.m.
Hi Eddy, thank you for the logs. I dug into it and I couldn't find a concrete reason why this could be happening on the app side. I looked through the MeshCore GitHub and found this issue, which sounds very much like what you're experiencing. The suggested workaround at the moment is to do a full flash erase your Heltec v3. After doing the full flash erase, you hopefully won't run into this issue anymore because a fix to prevent the issue was added in 1.13.0 https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/issues/1519
Heltec V3 companion regularily looses all contacts running Firmware...
This is a severe bug, but I don&#39;t see it reported as a issue yet: My Heltec V3 companions (companion-v1.11.0) keep loosing their contact lists. I&#39;m able to restore it by importing a saved c...
avion
Hi Eddy, thank you for the logs. I dug into it and I couldn't find a concrete reason why this could be happening on the app side. I looked through the MeshCore GitHub and found this issue, which sounds very much like what you're experiencing. The suggested workaround at the moment is to do a full flash erase your Heltec v3. After doing the full flash erase, you hopefully won't run into this issue anymore because a fix to prevent the issue was added in 1.13.0 https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/issues/1519
Eddy 2026-03-09 2:43 p.m.
Thanks @avion I will do the full flash erase.

Using my custom T-Deck Pro WiFi companion with Pocket Mesh on iOS and I'm a big fan of the way it reconnects immediately if I open the app. Thank you for making that happen!
5:57 a.m.
Did find a bug though - connecting to my node on 192.168.1.113, but Pocket Mesh records it as 112. This shows up as 192.168.1.112 in the app and in Previously Paired, though the node is definitely on 113

Did find a bug though - connecting to my node on 192.168.1.113, but Pocket Mesh records it as 112. This shows up as 192.168.1.112 in the app and in Previously Paired, though the node is definitely on 113
avion 2026-03-10 3:07 p.m.
that is odd lol. Thanks for the report, I'll look into it
avion
0.10.0 has been submitted for review! It takes about a full business day to pass review.

What's Changed

  • Connect Device: One less tap to connect—simply tap on your device. Long press nodes to edit or remove them.
  • Live Activity: View your node’s connection status, packets per minute, and battery level at a glance. Enabled by default (disable in Settings).
  • Settings: Added direct link to system Settings to change the app language.
  • Advanced Settings: Import a pre-generated private key or generate your own. Optionally generate keys with a specific prefix.
  • Chat: @mention suggestions now sorted by most recent sender instead of alphabetically.
  • Chat: Detect malicious links and warn the user
  • Chat: Send button now has a 1-second cooldown after sending a message.
  • Chat: On iPad, the sheet that opens when long pressing on a message is now taller by default
  • Chat List: Added persistent filter tabs at the top of the Chats list and removed the filter button.
  • Node Avatars: Now shows emoji if name contains one.
  • Repeater Admin: Added “Contact Info” section.
  • Message Paths: Path names in long-press menus now resolve discovered node names when no contact exists.
  • Trace Path Tool: New “include discovered” toggle to select from nearby discovered nodes.
  • Trace Path Tool: Rooms now appear in the trace path list.
  • Discover: Discovered node rows now show the node ID hash for easier identification.
  • Node Discovery: Add discovered repeaters right from the tool. No need to go to Discovery to add. ### Bug Fixes
  • Fixed text input not clearing occasionally after sending a message.
  • Fixed crash in LoS tool when nodes are too spread out.
  • Removed accidental ability to chat with Room nodes.
  • Fixed Radio Button visual glitch on iPadOS 26.
  • Fix rooms saying "Connected" when the radio was manually disconnected
  • Centered emoji row in message long press sheet
  • Fix T1000-E not matching to OCV preset
avion 2026-03-10 3:08 p.m.
0.10.0 has been approved finally! Let me know if you find any bugs
3:09 p.m.
0.10.1 was submitted for review a couple days ago and is currently in review by Apple, so likely less than 24 hrs till it hits TestFlight.

What's Changed

  • Reactions: Support for rendering (not sending) MeshCore Open reactions
  • Chat: Improved message bubble spacing in chats
  • Chat: Tell at a glance if the current chat is encrypted or not by looking at the new padlock in the input bar
  • Location permissions: GPS source now shown in settings with an alert when location access is denied
  • Onboarding: Check if device is connected to another app and warn user if detected
  • Connect Device: Allow connecting to another device while app is attempting to reconnect to a different node
  • Protocol: Added support for selecting Path Hash Size in Advanced Settings
  • Protocol: Added support for setting Auto-Add Max Hop Limit in Advanced Settings ### Bug Fixes
  • Fixed devices without GPS being unable to use the phone's GPS to update their location
  • Fixed message ordering so messages sort by receive time correctly
  • Fixed channel row disappearing before MeshCore device confirmation completed
  • Fixed invalid placeholder text in trace path code input
  • Removed "time since disconnected" from Live Activity
  • Added missing alert title when channel deletion fails
  • Made repeater telemetry and admin sheet data fetches more reliable
  • Fixed multiple bluetooth devices appearing in picker
4
2
2
3
2
1
2
2
avion 2026-03-10 3:16 p.m.
Well that was fast. 0.10.1 is now available
Eddy 2026-03-10 3:40 p.m.
3:40 p.m.
Yep
avion changed the channel name: MeshCore One - An open source MeshCore client for iOS/iPadOS 2026-03-10 4:56 p.m.
avion 2026-03-10 5:00 p.m.
PocketMesh is now known as MeshCore One! (Abbreviated as MC1) I'm a little sad to see the old name go, but it was getting confused with the Heltec MeshPocket. Also, iPads (and in the future Macs) don't fit that well in pockets Build 0.10.2 has been submitted to TestFlight to reflect this name and icon change. (edited)

Okay internal testing group hates the new icon So I’m going back to the drawing board on that. So if you hate the new icon once you get 0.10.2 then don’t fret, you’re not alone haha (edited)
DeKo xD 2026-03-11 11:46 a.m.
One problem I’ve found: received messages with the same hash (usually multiple paths, sometimes retries) are shown separately. I’d recommend:
  • grouping messages with same hash
  • showing a list of paths in the message details
  • when you tick to show the path and hop count in the message, show that info from the first message received
DeKo xD
One problem I’ve found: received messages with the same hash (usually multiple paths, sometimes retries) are shown separately. I’d recommend:
  • grouping messages with same hash
  • showing a list of paths in the message details
  • when you tick to show the path and hop count in the message, show that info from the first message received
avion 2026-03-11 6:01 p.m.
Hm, it should already be de-duplicating messages with the same hash. Could you DM me a screenshot of the RX Log and the messages that are showing separately?

avion
Hm, it should already be de-duplicating messages with the same hash. Could you DM me a screenshot of the RX Log and the messages that are showing separately?

DeKo xD 2026-03-12 1:07 p.m.

avion 2026-03-12 1:18 p.m.

1
DeKo xD 2026-03-14 8:35 a.m.
I was seeing the PRs. What’s the advantage of MapLibre over MapKit?
8:37 a.m.
I guess it’s offline maps
DeKo xD
I was seeing the PRs. What’s the advantage of MapLibre over MapKit?
avion 2026-03-15 1:44 a.m.
Yes, mainly offline maps. As far as I can tell, there isn't a supported way to do offline maps in MapKit. MapLibre is also going to allow us to get topography maps. MapLibre maps aren't as pretty as Apple's unfortunately. But for MeshCore, I think offline map capability is more important.
avion
Yes, mainly offline maps. As far as I can tell, there isn't a supported way to do offline maps in MapKit. MapLibre is also going to allow us to get topography maps. MapLibre maps aren't as pretty as Apple's unfortunately. But for MeshCore, I think offline map capability is more important.
DeKo xD 2026-03-15 9:15 a.m.
It’s unfortunate to lose the gorgeous MapKit maps, that make the app look very iOS native. But I totally agree, being offline is the key premise of MC.
avion
Haha yes, not yet. Hopefully tomorrow
pelgraine 2026-03-16 4:37 a.m.
Is there a plan to implement custom contacts adding settings? At the moment we just have manual or auto add all
pelgraine
Is there a plan to implement custom contacts adding settings? At the moment we just have manual or auto add all
avion 2026-03-16 3:48 p.m.
If your companion is on firmware 1.12 or higher, you should see "Selected Types" as an option. Is that what you're looking for? (edited)
avion
If your companion is on firmware 1.12 or higher, you should see "Selected Types" as an option. Is that what you're looking for? (edited)
pelgraine 2026-03-16 4:04 p.m.

pelgraine
It may be because I'm using custom firmware. I'll boot up and test with some other nodes but at the moment all I've got is Manual or All
avion 2026-03-16 4:10 p.m.
What firmware version does it show in Settings > Device Info?
avion
What firmware version does it show in Settings > Device Info?
pelgraine 2026-03-16 4:16 p.m.
It's custom firmware so probably something I have to fix on my end to get it to sync properly. Will give it a go with a v4 as well and report back
1
pelgraine
It's custom firmware so probably something I have to fix on my end to get it to sync properly. Will give it a go with a v4 as well and report back
avion 2026-03-16 4:18 p.m.
Ah I see. The selections in that menu are gated behind specific version numbers, so you may need to sync it up with MeshCore versions, or at least make it higher than them. There are several places in the app that have features gated based on official MeshCore firmware versions. (edited)
avion
Ah I see. The selections in that menu are gated behind specific version numbers, so you may need to sync it up with MeshCore versions, or at least make it higher than them. There are several places in the app that have features gated based on official MeshCore firmware versions. (edited)
pelgraine 2026-03-16 4:19 p.m.
Ooo very interesting, thank you
1
avion 2026-03-16 4:19 p.m.
You're welcome!
1
Gilles 2026-03-17 11:42 p.m.
This is just a little message to say thank you for all the time you are spending to make this wonderful app. We need this kind of polished UX so much. The official app (and globally the whole MeshCore look and feel) has yet to build an attractive visual identity and you are making big steps in that direction.
1
Gilles
This is just a little message to say thank you for all the time you are spending to make this wonderful app. We need this kind of polished UX so much. The official app (and globally the whole MeshCore look and feel) has yet to build an attractive visual identity and you are making big steps in that direction.
avion 2026-03-18 1:39 a.m.
I appreciate Liam's app, it follows the Android design style and is quick and easy to find settings and tools once you're used to it. I think it's great for power users. But it does feel quite out of place on Apple devices since it uses Android UI elements, and that's the niche I was aiming to fill with MC1. I'm super happy to hear you like it, makes all that time I put in worth it (edited)
3
avion 2026-03-19 5:06 p.m.
Hi everyone, version 0.10.3 of MC1 was just submitted to TestFlight for review. This is mostly a bug fix release, but there are some nice quality of life changes too. It seems like the reviews are taking quite a long time now, so expect to wait up to a week for it to be approved.

What's Changed

  • Inserting reply quoted text is now off by default. You can re-enable it in Settings > Chats
  • Added Packets Sent/Received to History graphs in Repeater Telemetry
  • You can now search for nodes by typing their public key. This works in both the Nodes tab and in the Discover view.
  • The Nodes list is now sorted by the radio's internal Last Modified time, instead of Last Advert. This should make it less confusing to see why a node hasn't been auto deleted.
  • New Advanced Settings shortcut in the Radio Button menu
  • Slightly adjusted chat message bubble padding and spacing
  • Reduced memory usage in chat and conversation list views ## Bug Fixes
  • Fixed a crash that could occur on app launch when the database failed to initialize
  • Fixed chat messages remaining in input bar after sending
  • Fixed trace path decoding to use the correct encoding format and hash mode
  • Fixed several concurrency issues in chat message processing and conversation reloading
  • Fixed Export Debug Logs button not working reliably
  • Fixed selfInfo frame length validation (Thanks @robekl!)
  • Fixed a bug that could cause excessive Bluetooth traffic after receiving messages @robekl
  • Optimized RX Log pruning @robekl Thank you to those that have been sending such great feedback and bug reports! You're awesome.

avion 2026-03-20 10:10 a.m.

If talking about the name in system Settings > Bluetooth getting changed to “MeshCore Device”, that’s unfortunately not something I can control until I drop support for iOS 18. You can change it in MC1 Settings > Bluetooth > Change Display Name. (edited)

Neil 2026-03-24 5:26 a.m.
So far seems pretty solid, only thing that I've felt is missing compared to the normal Meshcore app is an equivalent to "Discover neighbours" when doing remote management of a repeater (which I think just asks neighbours to send out a zero-hop advert?). Also I think there's a button in App Store Connect to allow testing iPad apps on Apple Silicon Macs, I would totally be up for testing that if you can switch it on! (edited)
Neil 2026-03-24 5:57 a.m.
Oh, probably the other thing that would be nice is if you could see the incoming path in the Discover section, as right now it doesn't show how many hops or by which path an advert came in
may 2026-03-24 6:24 a.m.

6:25 a.m.
i was trying to contract someone and they didn’t see my advert until i switched back to the main app
6:25 a.m.
unsure if that’s a coincidence or if adverts don’t work, but either way a confirmation would be a nice to have
Neil
So far seems pretty solid, only thing that I've felt is missing compared to the normal Meshcore app is an equivalent to "Discover neighbours" when doing remote management of a repeater (which I think just asks neighbours to send out a zero-hop advert?). Also I think there's a button in App Store Connect to allow testing iPad apps on Apple Silicon Macs, I would totally be up for testing that if you can switch it on! (edited)
avion 2026-03-24 7:55 p.m.
Ah yes, I forgot to add that. Will be added soon! Unfortunately the way I'm using to pair bluetooth devices (AccessorySetupKit) isn't available on macOS, so the iPad version can't pair with BLE radios on macOS at this point. I wanted to wait and create a full macOS native version of MC1 after 1.0 was released, but I'm weighing whether maintaining another client would be worth it or not.
Neil
Oh, probably the other thing that would be nice is if you could see the incoming path in the Discover section, as right now it doesn't show how many hops or by which path an advert came in
avion 2026-03-24 7:56 p.m.
Good point, I'll add this too!

avion 2026-03-24 7:57 p.m.

avion 2026-03-24 7:57 p.m.
If you happen to have an observer in your area (or another companion), you could look at the packets/RX log when you send adverts from MC1. Adverts work for me, but if you can't see the adverts in the analyzer/other app's RX Log, then please let me know! (edited)

usulsuspct 2026-03-27 7:35 a.m.
Is there anyway to “disconnect” and stay disconnected from a radio? Sometimes I need to connect to a radio from a different device, I thought you used to be able to simply disconnect?
Neil 2026-03-27 8:10 a.m.
I think I've noticed a bug with the "View Path", it doesn't seem to be deterministic on which node names it tries to match up to which partial key bytes. For further away nodes I guess there is no way it can, but for ones that are nearer, I would expect it to pick by distance
usulsuspct
Is there anyway to “disconnect” and stay disconnected from a radio? Sometimes I need to connect to a radio from a different device, I thought you used to be able to simply disconnect?
avion 2026-03-27 9:55 a.m.
If you tap "Disconnect" in the upper left button, it should stay disconnected.
Neil
I think I've noticed a bug with the "View Path", it doesn't seem to be deterministic on which node names it tries to match up to which partial key bytes. For further away nodes I guess there is no way it can, but for ones that are nearer, I would expect it to pick by distance
avion 2026-03-27 9:56 a.m.
Currently it does try to match by distance, but only via your phone's GPS. I'm not sure why I didn't add a check against the MeshCore device's set location, but I'll add that in the next version. (edited)
avion
If you tap "Disconnect" in the upper left button, it should stay disconnected.

avion
Currently it does try to match by distance, but only via your phone's GPS. I'm not sure why I didn't add a check against the MeshCore device's set location, but I'll add that in the next version. (edited)
Neil 2026-03-27 9:57 a.m.
Ah ha, fair enough, thank you!

avion 2026-03-27 10:09 a.m.
ahh the dynamic text size. hmm... I wonder if there's a way I can fix that without removing a menu item Maybe I can re-organize it so that Change Device and Disconnect are under the battery icon. Then only Advanced Settings would be at the very bottom.
Neil 2026-03-27 10:12 a.m.
You might also be able to set .dynamicTypeSize(...) on the popover menu, but yeah, rearranging the order seems logical too (edited)
avion 2026-03-27 10:12 a.m.
I originally put them at the very bottom assuming that they'd be the least used buttons. But I failed to consider that many users have multiple devices and want to use multiple apps. So I think reorganizing to put them at the top would make sense
Neil
You might also be able to set .dynamicTypeSize(...) on the popover menu, but yeah, rearranging the order seems logical too (edited)
avion 2026-03-27 10:13 a.m.
True. I do think it's a bit odd just how large that dynamic text is compared to other text on screen. Maybe I'll play around with .dynamicTypeSize and the order.
usulsuspct 2026-03-27 10:18 a.m.
Great - thanks for the consideration.
avion 2026-03-27 10:19 a.m.
of course! Thanks for the feedback
Neil 2026-03-27 10:19 a.m.
Something else also that I was wondering, but others may have a better idea on. The four bars showing signal strength in e.g. the neighbours list, they seem a bit... punishing? A 4.5dB SNR shows as red & only 2 bars, yet I would think by LoRa standards that's fairly reasonable
avion 2026-03-27 10:21 a.m.
Ah yes, that is something that I've been meaning to research further. I'm not sure what the correct ranges for quality are in LoRA. Wasn't super high on my priority list since it also displays SNR (edited)
Neil 2026-03-27 10:22 a.m.
I would think above +6dB is excellent, above 0dB is good, above -6dB is fair, below that is poor, but would be interested if others think the same
Neil 2026-03-27 12:22 p.m.
Did a thing: https://github.com/Avi0n/MeshCoreOne/pull/269
Adjust and unify SNR thresholds by neilalexander · Pull Request #2...
This changes the scaling of the SNR thresholds a bit and also lines up snrFormatted with SNRQuality.

pelgraine
Not nearly as badly. As I understand it, Meshcore One used a different connection method on iOS - in my experience it's been much more consistent about Bluetooth (and WiFi) stability
Skye 2026-03-27 3:32 p.m.
That's great to hear, I just wish it wasn't AI coded. Sorry personal preference
Skye
That's great to hear, I just wish it wasn't AI coded. Sorry personal preference
avion 2026-03-27 3:35 p.m.
Yup totally fair. I know it's strange coming from me, but I prefer projects that aren't AI coded as well and straight up skip repos that look vibe coded. That being said, an Apple native MeshCore client was something I personally wanted so figured I'd share what I had just in case people wanted to check it out. But yeah, that's why I'm very up front about it and put the disclaimer. I really dislike when project authors hide that they used AI.
2
2
Skye 2026-03-27 3:51 p.m.
For sure appreciate that, and good to get the ball rolling to gauge interest before spending a lot of time learning Swift etc. it has a lot of potential
Uncle Lit 2026-03-29 12:37 p.m.
I am asking this for all companion applications. could you make sending messages in multi-byte option per channel and per contact? I imagine for a while even after all repeaters are updated to 1.14+, there will be channels you want to send with 1-byte to get 64 hops, and then there will be channels you want to send with 2- or 3-byte to disembogue repeater IDs and fewer hops. Another example: as a regional mesh updating their repeaters to 1.14+, I may want to create a #multibytepublic hashtag channel to test multibyte with others in public. We can do this to test 1.14 repeater firmware take rate and coverage. We would need to send only multibyte messages in #multibyte_public, but keep regular pubilc channel in 1-byte path hash.
Uncle Lit
I am asking this for all companion applications. could you make sending messages in multi-byte option per channel and per contact? I imagine for a while even after all repeaters are updated to 1.14+, there will be channels you want to send with 1-byte to get 64 hops, and then there will be channels you want to send with 2- or 3-byte to disembogue repeater IDs and fewer hops. Another example: as a regional mesh updating their repeaters to 1.14+, I may want to create a #multibytepublic hashtag channel to test multibyte with others in public. We can do this to test 1.14 repeater firmware take rate and coverage. We would need to send only multibyte messages in #multibyte_public, but keep regular pubilc channel in 1-byte path hash.
avion 2026-03-29 9:06 p.m.
I really like the idea, but I'm not comfortable with adding it at the app layer since there's no easy way to change the path hash mode dynamically per channel. I believe the only way at the moment is to set the firmware setting which would lead to having to do the following for every message sent in a channel with a different path hash mode set: (Assume starting in 1 byte mode)
  • Change path hash to 2 bytes (for example)
  • Wait for .ok from firmware
  • Send message
  • Change path hash back to 1 byte
  • Wait for .ok from firmware If this feature ever lands in the firmware, I'd be super happy to implement it in MC1. But until then, I don't think it would be wise to do everything at the app layer. But if there's something I'm misunderstanding about how the firmware works, I'm all ears.
 (edited)
1
avion
I really like the idea, but I'm not comfortable with adding it at the app layer since there's no easy way to change the path hash mode dynamically per channel. I believe the only way at the moment is to set the firmware setting which would lead to having to do the following for every message sent in a channel with a different path hash mode set: (Assume starting in 1 byte mode)
  • Change path hash to 2 bytes (for example)
  • Wait for .ok from firmware
  • Send message
  • Change path hash back to 1 byte
  • Wait for .ok from firmware If this feature ever lands in the firmware, I'd be super happy to implement it in MC1. But until then, I don't think it would be wise to do everything at the app layer. But if there's something I'm misunderstanding about how the firmware works, I'm all ears.
 (edited)
Uncle Lit 2026-03-29 9:41 p.m.
that is also what Liam said when I posted this question to #meshcore-app channel. It makes sense to have the firmware manage that.
Silas 2026-03-30 2:08 a.m.
Hi @avion I am on MeshCore for 2 days and testing MeshCore One for 1 day now — so maybe I miss something, bare with me First of all, I really enjoy using the app, great UX, very meaningful information everywhere! Also I am looking forward to the topographical maps for their information density. Two minor improvements, I’d like to ask for would be:
  • ability to show a repeater in the map straight from the message path shown in a chat (or maybe even all known repeaters in the path — would be even better imho)
  • display of altitude together with lat/long If you are open for PRs I might have a look at it myself, if I find the time
Silas
Hi @avion I am on MeshCore for 2 days and testing MeshCore One for 1 day now — so maybe I miss something, bare with me First of all, I really enjoy using the app, great UX, very meaningful information everywhere! Also I am looking forward to the topographical maps for their information density. Two minor improvements, I’d like to ask for would be:
  • ability to show a repeater in the map straight from the message path shown in a chat (or maybe even all known repeaters in the path — would be even better imho)

Hi, thanks for testing MC1! Really happy to hear you're enjoying it
  • This is planned, but not until after 1.0.

And yes, totally open to PR's if they're discussed beforehand. If you'd like to take a crack at them feel free! (I'm sorry in advance about the code base...)
1
1
10:46 a.m.

Version 0.11.0 has been submitted to TestFlight and is currently in review!

This release includes some very large changes under the hood: Maps and Room Admin UI. I didn't have a room with admin access to test with (I really need to buy another test device..), so any and all feedback would be appreciated on this! It should work in theory, but there will probably be a lot more jank/broken things than normal.

What's Changed

  • Offline maps are now available. Find it in Settings > Offline Maps. To support this feature, all maps have moved from Apple Maps to MapLibre.
  • Line-of-Sight tool now puts red X markers on the map where terrain blocks the signal.
  • Channels can be scoped to a region from the channel info sheet. A new Region Management view lets you manage known regions.
  • Repeater admins can trigger neighbor discovery from the status view.
  • Region configuration is available in repeater settings.
  • Non-admin users can connect to a repeater in guest mode and see its status without admin credentials.
  • Room server nodes get the same management UI that repeaters have.
  • Add contacts by pasting a meshcore:// URL. Tap paste on the Add Contact sheet and the name, key, and type fill in automatically.
  • Added Receive Errors to Telemetry history graphs.
  • See repeater/room history without logging in. A Saved History button on repeater/room contacts opens charts grouped by battery, SNR, packet counts, and errors.
  • If your phone phone doesn't have GPS or you haven't given the app location permissions, the app now uses the radio's location (if set) for distance calculations, hop sorting, and map centering.
  • Nodes found in Discovery show hop count and routing path.
  • Recalibrated signal quality thresholds (thanks @neilalexander).
  • Messages from blocked contacts and channel senders are now deleted instead of hidden
  • Re-organized Radio Menu Button in upper left to move Change Device and Disconnect closer to the top
 (edited)
3
10:46 a.m.

Bug Fixes

  • Concurrent commands could receive each other's responses, causing silent failures or wrong data (thanks @robekl).
  • Fixed keyboard staying visible after sending the first message in a conversation.
  • Incoming messages using hash byte other than 1 showed the wrong hop count instead of "direct." Hop counts display correctly now.
  • Batteryless and mains-powered devices stop triggering false low battery alerts.
  • Signal quality colors match across the map and the rest of the app.
  • Telemetry chart x-axis dates are consistent (month/day only).
  • Room server status data parses correctly (thanks @robekl).
  • Radios with private key export disabled no longer hang the app (thanks @robekl).
  • Fixed not being able to backspace text in Repeater Info
 (edited)
1
avion 2026-03-30 10:55 a.m.

avion 2026-03-30 4:16 p.m.

avion 2026-03-30 4:16 p.m.

Skye
In radio Advanced Settings under Hash Size it should probably mention "Repeaters running pre-v1.14.0 firmware will not repeat any messages with hash size > 1 byte"
avion 2026-03-30 4:23 p.m.
I initially didn't put that explanation there because I figured someone changing that setting would know. But you're right, it would better to have that info up front. Will add that, thanks! (edited)
Skye 2026-03-30 4:24 p.m.
0.11.0 looking really good

avion 2026-03-30 4:25 p.m.
It got approved already? Wow that was fast.
4:27 p.m.
0.11.0 is probably more buggy than usual because so much of the code was refactored to swap to MapLibre and unify repeater/room admin UI. So let me know if you run into anything, my note pad is ready to scribble down the bugs haha (edited)
Skye 2026-03-30 4:45 p.m.
Can you do 2 and 4 byte traces or just 1 byte right now?
Skye
Can you do 2 and 4 byte traces or just 1 byte right now?
avion 2026-03-30 4:48 p.m.
The code is there to do 2 and 3 byte traces if you've changed your radio's hash size setting. But I don't have enough repeaters around me on 1.14, so it's untested at the moment.
Skye 2026-03-30 4:49 p.m.
Trace predates multibyte path, it uses a different method using bitshift so the possible values are 1,2,4 bytes. The payload contains the path, the header path is overloaded to store RSSI/SNR values it's a bit messy
4:50 p.m.
Not documented anywhere that I can tell, just from what I've seen in the dev chat and looking at the fw code
avion 2026-03-30 4:50 p.m.
ah I hadn't heard of that. In that case, no it doesn't support that
Neil 2026-03-30 4:56 p.m.
The new UI changes look good, but it certainly feels like it’s disconnecting and reconnecting from Bluetooth to my Heltec v3 more often than it was
avion 2026-03-30 4:56 p.m.
Compared to the previous version?
Neil 2026-03-30 4:57 p.m.
Yep
5:00 p.m.
I’ll try getting closer to the companion for a bit and see if it persists
avion 2026-03-30 5:03 p.m.
What are you doing when it disconnects/reconnects? I'm wondering if maybe ESP32 devices don't play as well as nrf with the new request serializer.
Neil 2026-03-30 5:08 p.m.
I was looking at telemetry and neighbours etc of my repeater
5:08 p.m.
So there were probably quite a few requests in a short period of time
avion 2026-03-30 5:09 p.m.
Hm that might be it then. I'll look into that
1
Skye 2026-03-31 12:35 a.m.
Bluetooth stays connected and it’s not draining my phone battery that’s a big win
1
12:36 a.m.
Is there a repeater admin cli somewhere?
Skye
Is there a repeater admin cli somewhere?
avion 2026-03-31 1:02 a.m.
Yes, in the Tools tab. It's quite different from Liam's app. I hadn't used his CLI before I made MC1's and I got a little carried away making it feel like a terminal... To login to a repeater/room, you need to type login REPEATER_NAME
1:03 a.m.
There is a tab button on the far left. You can use it for tab suggestions. If you see a ghost text suggestion, you can press the right arrow to accept it.
1:04 a.m.
The CLI also keeps your place. So if you login to a repeater, you can back out to message someone, for example, and if you return to the CLI you can pick up where you left off. But I understand most people probably don't like it being a completely separate tool and expect it to live in the repeater/room's info page. So eventually I'm going to refactor the CLI to allow that functionality. (edited)
Skye 2026-03-31 1:41 a.m.
Thanks! I'll give it a try, doesn't have to be a copy of Liam's app
avion 2026-03-31 1:46 a.m.
If I do it, I'd still keep the CLI as a separate tool. But make it a reusable View so that I can make it pop up in a Sheet when someone presses the CLI button in the repeater/room's node info page. This would have the advantage of not having to go to the Tools tab and type login .... But if I do decide to make this change, it won't get done till after 1.0 is released most likely. (edited)
1
avion
I like it. If you tackle it, make sure to have a fallback for when offline. In the Line of Sight tool, I use Open-Meteo for elevation data. Including distance sounds cool too!

Silas
TBH I assumed altitude to be part of the GPS data and transmitted over the mesh. Personally I would not make web requests for it. I will have a look at it but don’t want to promise anything. Build 108 is now available in TestFlight. You won't see your version number change in the app or in TestFlight, but think of this as 0.11.1
  • Added Airtime to Repeater/Room Telemetry Status view
  • Fix Line of Sight tool's height adjustment not working
  • Fix crash that could happen when discovering regions
  • Potential fix for ESP32 devices disconnecting more often

Silas 2026-04-01 2:14 a.m.

Neil 2026-04-01 6:29 a.m.
Feels quite a bit better so far
Eddy
(My app still has all its content after the update) (edited)
Silas 2026-04-01 3:01 p.m.
Hmm strange. Thanks for your report. Will have look how it will be next time.
Silas
Thanks. I realized app state was cleared after the update (like received messages). Is this intentional during the beta?
avion 2026-04-01 3:07 p.m.
Did you happen to install the app via Xcode and then delete it? If you delete the app it removes the BLE device from iOS. And currently deleting the device causes all the data associated with it to be removed. So if you then opened the TestFlight app, it would see that the device was deleted from the system and cascade delete all data associated with it so it isn't orphaned. It makes developing a bit of a pain unless you have a separate device for testing though. I want to eventually create an app backup feature to export stored messages, discovered nodes, etc. because I do think this is a pain point.
1
3:07 p.m.
But if you only had the TestFlight version installed, I may have messed up something in the db related to blocking senders/users... Let me know if that's the case. (edited)
Neil
Feels quite a bit better so far
avion 2026-04-01 3:09 p.m.
Yay! Keep an eye on it and let me know if it seems to still be a regression. If it continues, could you DM me a debug log export after the disconnect/reconnect occurs?
Skye 2026-04-01 3:44 p.m.
Is there a way to share your public key or advert info in a channel or DM that someone can tap on and add you as a contact without receiving an advert? I thought I saw something about that maybe in meshcore:// format
3:50 p.m.
If the app registered as a handler for that url format you could also share pubkeys out of band ie Signal and would open the app and add contact very useful for long distance contacts
Skye
Is there a way to share your public key or advert info in a channel or DM that someone can tap on and add you as a contact without receiving an advert? I thought I saw something about that maybe in meshcore:// format
avion 2026-04-01 3:52 p.m.
There isn't at the moment, I should add sharing meshcore:// format for your own contact.
1
1
Skye
If the app registered as a handler for that url format you could also share pubkeys out of band ie Signal and would open the app and add contact very useful for long distance contacts
avion 2026-04-01 3:53 p.m.
Unfortunately I can't do that since Liam's app is registered as meshcore://. I could make MC1 share it's own mc1:// but then only MC1 apps would understand it
Skye 2026-04-01 3:58 p.m.
I have multiple browsers on my phone surely they're all registered as https handlers, doesn't iOS just let you pick?
avion 2026-04-01 3:59 p.m.
Browsers are different because Apple lets you choose default apps to open URLs. But there's no way to set a default app for MeshCore. Liam already has meshcore:// registered with Apple so that will always open his app (or nothing if it isn't installed).
Skye 2026-04-01 4:00 p.m.
Ah bummer thanks
avion 2026-04-01 4:00 p.m.
Yeah I wish we could set default apps for everything like Android
Skye 2026-04-01 4:01 p.m.
Maybe just match any valid length hex string in channel or DM? Make it clickable "Would you like to add as a contact"
4:01 p.m.
All you need is pubkey right
avion 2026-04-01 4:01 p.m.
Yep that would probably work. Would have to think through if there are security implications with that, but I can't think of any off the top of my head...
Skye 2026-04-01 4:02 p.m.
Maybe DM only? but that would require a trusted 3rd party
4:02 p.m.
or private channel
avion 2026-04-01 4:03 p.m.
Yeah if I run into any security implications with the parser then that would be a good fallback
Skye 2026-04-01 4:16 p.m.
Looks like meshcore:// format includes the full advert data so probably better to go with that
avion 2026-04-01 4:18 p.m.
I'm not 100% sure I can override the OS's deep linking, but yeah that is my preferred method. Since at the moment you can already copy the public key and add a contact that way.
Skye 2026-04-01 4:19 p.m.
Not as a handler but just detect the string in channels and DMs within the app and make it clickable. And the ability to generate & copy to clipboard in the advert menu
avion 2026-04-01 4:19 p.m.
mhm that's what I'm talking about as well. But if I can't override the deep linking then tapping it would always try to open Liam's app
Skye 2026-04-01 4:20 p.m.
ohh gotcha
avion 2026-04-01 4:20 p.m.
it's possible I can turn it off, not sure yet
4:21 p.m.
hah, I should've tested it first instead of assuming. Receiving a meshcore:// advert text doesn't make it a tappable deep link, so this should be doable! (edited)

avion
Yay! Keep an eye on it and let me know if it seems to still be a regression. If it continues, could you DM me a debug log export after the disconnect/reconnect occurs?
Neil 2026-04-01 4:31 p.m.

1
avion 2026-04-01 4:31 p.m.
I take it back, what I said above isn't true. Apple doesn't enforce uniqueness for custom URL schemes such as meshcore:// so I can make MC1 open it. https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xcode/defining-a-custom-url-scheme-for-your-app (edited)
Defining a custom URL scheme for your app | Apple Developer Documen...
Use specially formatted URLs to link to content within your app.
4:32 p.m.
But if multiple apps have the same custom URL scheme then it isn't possible to determine which app will actually open the URL. So if you have both Liam's app and MC1 and they both use meshcore:// as their URL, then it would be random which one would open lol (edited)
4:37 p.m.
I see. I was confusing custom URL schemes with universal links. What I said above is true for universal links, but not custom URL schemes https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xcode/allowing-apps-and-websites-to-link-to-your-content
Allowing apps and websites to link to your content | Apple Develope...
Use universal links to link directly to content within your app and share data securely.
Skye 2026-04-01 7:09 p.m.
Interesting thanks for the deep dive
7:12 p.m.
I see so universal links work off https://domain so it can fall back to browser if there's no app installed (edited)
Jude
Fully built with AI is impressive nicley done
bye andy 2026-04-02 3:20 a.m.
what a great way to end up with security vulnerabilities

Did you happen to install the app via Xcode and then delete it? If you delete the app it removes the BLE device from iOS. And currently deleting the device causes all the data associated with it to be removed. So if you then opened the TestFlight app, it would see that the device was deleted from the system and cascade delete all data associated with it so it isn't orphaned. It makes developing a bit of a pain unless you have a separate device for testing though. I want to eventually create an app backup feature to export stored messages, discovered nodes, etc. because I do think this is a pain point.

my initial feedback:
  • it is a bit more unreliable than the official app. BLE doesn't seem to stay connected. and even when it is connected it seems to miss notifications. if i open it says it try reconnecting but then cannot. i have to restart the app to get it working again
  • notifications i do get just say the name of the app instead of the data from the notification. dunno if this is app specific but i'd rather just see the <client name>: <message>

  • seems like there is enough space at top for dedicated quick options such as advert, toggle GPS, settings, etc

  • notifications i do get just say the name of the app instead of the data from the notification. dunno if this is app specific but i'd rather just see the <client name>: <message>

1
avion 2026-04-03 6:37 p.m.

Build 109 is now available

This build introduces a new BLE throttler for ESP32 based devices to fix an issue where if the BLE link got saturated, MC1 would continue to flood it instead of backing off. This is likely what was causing some sync issues for ESP32 devices. I don't have an ESP32 device so please let me know if this helps (or makes things worse).

What's Changed

  • You can now fetch telemetry from companion type nodes. Minimally tested, please let me know any feedback you have on this! Even simple stuff like, "Why is there a blank row here?"
  • Sharing a contact via advert button now has a green confirmation checkmark when it succeeds
  • Added ability to change Direct Message Multi Acks in Advanced Settings
  • Hide Channel 0 when fetching telemetry ## Bug Fixes
  • Sharing a contact via advert when the node's advertisement data was missing or too old would show the wrong error message
  • New BLE throttler for ESP32 based devices to prevent saturating the device's link
  • Fixed a couple map coordinate bounding issues that caused crashes when loading nodes with invalid coordinates
 (edited)
2
Gilles 2026-04-05 9:38 a.m.
Hi! I just did a first test with a MeshCore sensor (so a node with a firmware compiled with the sensor flag). While your app can see it on the sensor scanner, it is impossible to add it to my node list. Then after adding my sensor using the official app I went back to yours and the sensor acted like any other contact (so no management option, no auto refresh telemetry, …) I know it is a forgotten feature of MeshCore but with devices like the T1000-E tracker it might be useful to setup trackers properly (instead of having a client wrongly used as a tracker). I don’t think it’s an urgent feature to add but it might be nice to have it on the todo list
gjelsoe 2026-04-06 7:20 a.m.
I guess that you already knows but it's kind of annoying to see messages like d1k21v6z for a non-MC1 user.
Lïttlëaton 2026-04-06 2:56 p.m.
Any thoughts of an auto split up messages feature? when sending long messges?
Eddy 2026-04-07 2:57 a.m.
Auto split up and long massages sound a bit strange with the purpose of MeshCore and the way MeshCore works.
Skye 2026-04-07 12:08 p.m.
Links aren't clickable in room server chat
Skye 2026-04-07 12:18 p.m.
Can't seem to long tap on room server messages either
Eddy
Auto split up and long massages sound a bit strange with the purpose of MeshCore and the way MeshCore works.
Lïttlëaton 2026-04-07 12:21 p.m.
hmm there is another app that does that somwhere.. not sure what its called. .always seeing replies/messages with text to the limit then 1/2, then more message then 2/2 etc..
Eddy 2026-04-07 12:35 p.m.
Probably not hard to build. I would think MeshCore isn’t meant to send long text. And you probably won’t receive all split messages.
1
Neil 2026-04-07 12:46 p.m.
Problem is that you have no idea whether your multiple messages will be delivered in order, take the same route or arrive at all
12:46 p.m.
It's not really something that is addressed at the protocol level, so addressing it in apps as hacky workarounds is going to perform badly and surprise people
1
Lïttlëaton
Any thoughts of an auto split up messages feature? when sending long messges?
avion 2026-04-07 1:05 p.m.
I likely won't implement this because I don't want to encourage long messages in MeshCore to save airtime. As far as possible, I'm trying to make MC1 be respectful of airtime and other clients. I realize I'm being a bit of a hypocrite here because I implemented reactions which aren't part of the protocol (and I am somewhat regretting implementing it now because it does use double the airtime). But at the time, this was something I felt would add to the experience without causing too much annoyance to others. For MC1 users it improves the experience since they can now see MeshCore Open's reactions. It is still annoying to other client users as @gjelsoe mentioned above, but at least it is readable. As soon as MeshCore implements reactions at the protocol level, I'll adopt it in MC1 and get rid of the custom implementation. As much as possible, I don't want to create app-side hacks for features the protocol is missing. So I probably won't add a feature to auto split messages, even though I have wished for that using MC1 myself.
2

avion 2026-04-07 1:07 p.m.
Thanks, I'll work on this soon. Currently trying to figure out a bluetooth bug so other features are on hold at the moment.

Skye 2026-04-09 8:10 p.m.
What is Max Flood Hops in repeater admin - Behavior? I wasn’t aware an advert TTL had been added maybe I missed it in the repeater release notes
8:14 p.m.
Huh it’s in config.meshcore.dev too nevermind not sure how I missed that
Skye
What is Max Flood Hops in repeater admin - Behavior? I wasn’t aware an advert TTL had been added maybe I missed it in the repeater release notes
avion 2026-04-09 8:17 p.m.
My understanding is that it controls what your repeater repeats. So if you have it set to only 8, for example, then it won't repeat a packet it receives that has already hopped 8 times. https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/blob/main/docs/cli_commands.md#limit-the-number-of-hops-for-a-flood-message (edited)
Skye 2026-04-09 8:23 p.m.
Weird I've never seen it mentioned before, looks like it was added over a year ago
avion 2026-04-09 8:24 p.m.
I think it's a CLI only command in Liam's app, so maybe not too much attention to it.
avion
I think it's a CLI only command in Liam's app, so maybe not too much attention to it.
Skye 2026-04-09 8:26 p.m.
Yeah that's what I'm thinking
avion 2026-04-09 8:44 p.m.

Build 113 is now available

This build contains syncing changes for both nrf52 and esp32 devices. Let me know if you notice any issues.

What's changed

  • You can now toggle Flood Routing when logging into a remote node
  • Scrolling through telemetry history charts is now much easier
  • Temperature, pressure, altitude, and distance now show in your locale's preferred units (Fahrenheit, inHg, feet for non-metric)
  • Voltage, current, and energy readings show three decimal places now (e.g. 3.742 V) to match chart precision
  • Deleting your device in System Bluetooth Settings no longer removes all associated data in MC1
  • "Forget Device" now offers two choices: unpair but keep your chat history and contacts, or unpair and delete everything
  • Channel invite links and contact share links in messages are tappable. Tap to join/add, or jump straight to the channel or contact if you already have them
  • Several English-only strings (import errors, auth failures, clearance labels, share sheet) are now translated into all 9 supported languages
8:44 p.m.

Bug fixes

  • Voltage charts no longer clip readings that fall outside the expected battery range
  • Devices running newer firmware (v9/v10) that send shorter device info packets no longer fail to connect
  • Starting a Wi-Fi session opened a second TCP connection, orphaning the one already established
  • Cancelling the Bluetooth PIN prompt no longer triggers a retry loop
  • Removing a device no longer leaves a dangling Bluetooth connection behind
  • DMs were silently failing to decrypt due to a key format mismatch, and DMs that arrived before contacts synced were permanently unreadable
  • Sent messages retried after a lost ACK no longer show up as duplicates
  • On first connection to v10+ devices, outgoing path length was encoded wrong until you reconnected
  • Channel message timestamps could get decoded with the wrong byte order, producing incorrect times or tripping replay protection
  • A failed sync no longer leaves the connection looking "Ready" when it isn't. The new "Syncing" state also closes a timing race where the resync loop could exit early and never recover.
  • A race condition could let two syncs run at the same time during connection
4
1
1
1
1
1
2
1
1
8:48 p.m.
Thank you to everyone who submitted TestFlight feedback and GH issues! You've been sending amazing feedback with annotated screenshots and everything. I really appreciate it

Jonathan 2026-04-15 5:16 p.m.
hello! If the “Last Advert” time is way off than what I’d expect, does that mean the repeaters clock is wrong, or is it a bug with the app?
5:16 p.m.
supposedly I saw this repeater recently, but May 16th is a long while ago. (edited)
Jonathan
hello! If the “Last Advert” time is way off than what I’d expect, does that mean the repeaters clock is wrong, or is it a bug with the app?
avion 2026-04-15 5:27 p.m.
It's a little confusing, but what happened is that you received an advert from that repeater recently, but their clock is way off. The Nodes tab used to show the Sent Time from the repeater's advert packet, but I changed it a bit ago to instead reflect the Last Modified timestamp on the companion radio to better reflect when your comanion last heard the advert.
Neil
One thing that would be nice is if “Edit Path” had a search/filter box, otherwise it’s an extremely long list to scroll up and down
avion 2026-04-15 5:28 p.m.
I'm planning on getting to this soon!
1
avion
It's a little confusing, but what happened is that you received an advert from that repeater recently, but their clock is way off. The Nodes tab used to show the Sent Time from the repeater's advert packet, but I changed it a bit ago to instead reflect the Last Modified timestamp on the companion radio to better reflect when your comanion last heard the advert.
Jonathan 2026-04-15 5:53 p.m.
ah perfect! that’s what I figured was happening - wasn’t sure. thanks!
1
5:54 p.m.

6:33 p.m.

avion 2026-04-21 3:11 a.m.

Build 119 is now available

The main feature in this release is Backup & Restore. This feature required a lot of background changes and data loss may occur. Please test and let me know if you experience any issues related to saved devices and/or data.

What's Changed

  • Backup/Restore feature. Back up all your app data including messages, notification settings, saved paths in the Trace Path Tool, etc. Import them into a completely separate iPhone/iPad if you'd like!
  • Send DMs to channel users by long pressing on their message
  • Room message URLs are now tappable
  • "Discover Regions" now automatically adds repeaters to your device's contact list if it finds a repeater with a region
  • Redesigned contact path editor. Now includes a search field and sort filters.
  • Added OCV curves for T-Beam 1W and ThinkNode M6 ## Known Issue
  • Filters in the "Add Hop" sheet are missing a background and are currently transparent
5

Neil 2026-04-21 7:00 p.m.
Something else I noticed is that there are occasions where the app reports the wrong path lengths when 2- or 3-byte IDs are present, I think when discovering paths, and also if you edit a path with one byte setting and then extend the path after having changed to another byte setting, you end up with mixed byte lengths in the path which then don’t work until you reset or rebuild the whole path

Neil

avion 2026-04-21 7:07 p.m.
Oh that's odd, it should be following local units.. I'll double check that. Eventually I plan on adding a setting for that as well.

Neil 2026-04-21 7:08 p.m.
I don’t recall if it was using °C on older builds or not, but it’s °F on the latest and I have absolutely no intuitive sense of Fahrenheit at all

7:10 p.m.

Neil

avion 2026-04-21 7:12 p.m.

avion
Is the CLI not feeling nice because of having to type login <repeater>? Or just more intuitive to have the CLI in the repeater/room management page? (edited)
Neil 2026-04-21 7:15 p.m.
Bit of both to be honest, it’s actually not clear what login expects (name, partial key, full key) and I suppose it would be nice if it were just shortcutted from remote management
7:16 p.m.
I also did switch to WiFi companion recently and the TCP mode works pretty well, only thing I’ve noticed is that it doesn’t make any attempt to maintain the connection in the background like it does with BLE, even with the live activity enabled. Entirely possible that it’s Apple being Apple though, as I know Bluetooth gets background processing time that apps don’t otherwise usually get
Neil
Bit of both to be honest, it’s actually not clear what login expects (name, partial key, full key) and I suppose it would be nice if it were just shortcutted from remote management
avion 2026-04-21 7:17 p.m.
Makes sense, I'll add that to my list to do before 1.0. In the mean time, pressing the Tab icon in the CLI gives you a list of suggested commands/repeater names
Neil
I also did switch to WiFi companion recently and the TCP mode works pretty well, only thing I’ve noticed is that it doesn’t make any attempt to maintain the connection in the background like it does with BLE, even with the live activity enabled. Entirely possible that it’s Apple being Apple though, as I know Bluetooth gets background processing time that apps don’t otherwise usually get
avion 2026-04-21 7:18 p.m.
Unfortunately it's an Apple limitation, they cut all TCP connections in the background
avion
